target.title: feat(deepseek): show provider balance in usage status
target.body:
## What

- Registers DeepSeek as a provider usage source and wires the DeepSeek provider plugin to resolve API-key usage auth.
- Adds a DeepSeek balance fetcher for `GET /user/balance`, exposed through the plugin SDK provider-usage subpath.
- Adds `ProviderUsageSnapshot.summary` so balance-only providers can report provider text without pretending it is a quota window.
- Shows summary-only usage in `/status`, status reports, and the models auth status usage enrichment for DeepSeek API-key profiles.

## Why

After the provider plugin architecture moved provider-specific usage logic into plugins, DeepSeek was not wired into usage snapshots. Users could see local session costs, but `/status` and related status surfaces could not show the DeepSeek account balance.

DeepSeek balance is not a reset-window quota, so this keeps it as provider-reported summary text instead of fabricating a percent-left window.

## Public Contract Rationale

- `ProviderUsageSnapshot.summary` is optional and additive. Existing providers with quota windows keep using `windows`; shared formatters only fall back to `summary` when a provider has no windows and no error.
- `models.authStatus.usage.summary` is optional and mirrors the same snapshot field so API-key balance providers can expose account-state text without changing existing window payloads.
- The provider-usage SDK export keeps DeepSeek-specific HTTP parsing inside the bundled DeepSeek provider path instead of requiring plugins to import internal `src` modules.
- Tests cover summary-only formatting, DeepSeek auth-status enrichment, provider loading, and existing window-based formatting behavior.

## Impact

DeepSeek API-key users can now see balance text such as `Balance ¥42.50` in usage/status surfaces. Existing quota-window providers continue to use `windows`; summary-only formatting is only used when no windows are present.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: DeepSeek API-key usage snapshots now return provider balance text and the summary-only formatter displays it instead of dropping providers with no quota windows.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS checkout of this PR branch at `797c52ea95070e09c84ece1df7463e169007a68e`, Node/tsx runtime, real DeepSeek API key from local secret storage. Secret and balance amount were redacted from the captured output.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local Node/tsx snippet that imports `fetchDeepSeekUsage` from `src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.deepseek.ts`, wraps `fetch` to capture only safe request metadata, calls the live DeepSeek `https://api.deepseek.com/user/balance` endpoint with the real API key, then passes the returned snapshot through `formatUsageWindowSummary`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
DeepSeek usage live proof
head=797c52ea95070e09c84ece1df7463e169007a68e
endpoint=https://api.deepseek.com/user/balance
method=GET
authorizationHeaderPresent=true
accept=application/json
provider=deepseek
displayName=DeepSeek
windows=0
hasSummary=true
summary=Balance ¥<redacted>
formatted=Balance ¥<redacted>
error=none
```

- Observed result after fix: The live DeepSeek response produced a `ProviderUsageSnapshot` with `provider=deepseek`, zero quota windows, a non-empty balance summary, and no error. The formatter returned the same redacted balance text instead of `null`.
- What was not tested: Full gateway UI/dashboard manual click path and invalid-key live error handling were not exercised in this live proof. The exact balance amount is intentionally redacted.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This uses a real DeepSeek account balance endpoint, but the local account balance value cannot be shared publicly. Automated unit tests cover the mocked error and formatting edge cases separately.
- Before evidence: Before this patch, DeepSeek was absent from `usageProviders` and had no provider usage hook, so `/status` and usage surfaces had no DeepSeek balance snapshot to format.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.deepseek.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.format.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.load.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.shared.test.ts extensions/deepseek/index.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts`
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/concepts/usage-tracking.md docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn-applies-thinking-default.test.ts src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Current Branch Note

This branch was refreshed onto current `upstream/main` (`95890fe150`) in merge commit `e2e13557ce0d43c6943ab274dbc3a85f5ea47aed`; follow-up commits `2aa4a69943` and `797c52ea95` only align subagent config typing and clarify usage docs.

A local `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen`/commit hook attempt was blocked by registry fetch timeouts while installing newly introduced upstream binary packages. The SDK baseline was regenerated and checked directly with `node --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts`; GitHub CI will re-run the full matrix on the pushed merge head.

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 10.33.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `25243d99b2009a0c437874ed68dca24ad8c8614c`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `pnpm openclaw agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-25243d99b2` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof.NRgAFc/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof.NRgAFc/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof pnpm openclaw agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-25243d99b2 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the CLI run and request-log assertion:

```text
[agent/embedded] [trace:embedded-run] startup stages: runId=c43c486b-23d0-4e83-8fd1-c0dd12f67cba sessionId=proof-subagent-session phase=attempt-dispatch totalMs=5818 stages=workspace:1ms@1ms,runtime-plugins:5605ms@5606ms,hooks:6ms@5612ms,model-resolution:113ms@5725ms,auth:67ms@5792ms,context-engine:1ms@5793ms,attempt-workspace:1ms@5794ms,attempt-prompt:0ms@5794ms,attempt-runtime-plan:24ms@5818ms,attempt-dispatch:0ms@5818ms
```

```json
{
  "head": "25243d99b2009a0c437874ed68dca24ad8c8614c",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-25243d99b2",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": ["exec", "read"],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported runtimes, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, and appears in model-facing dynamic tool snapshots.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native embedded subagent runs. Unsupported paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced.

## Repro + Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 5 files, 159 tests, 3 Vitest shards.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...changed files...` passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` passed: `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=dot src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` passed locally after CI showed a timeout/interrupt flake in that unrelated file: 1 file, 48 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed before the snapshot commit; `pnpm openclaw agent --help` rebuilt dist successfully after the snapshot commit because the CLI detected a new git head.
- Current-head CLI/mock OpenAI proof above passed.

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, session persistence, embedded command runner propagation, persisted child-session allowlist inheritance, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist behavior, prompt snapshot update, formatter/typecheck/build/whitespace checks.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.
- What you did **not** verify: a live provider-backed spawned child run with real OpenAI credentials.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: fix(ios): subscribe to per-session transcripts so group chats update in real time (#80231)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #80231 — Aight (iOS) group chats never surface assistant replies in real time. The user must exit the chat and re-enter for the next agent message to appear. 1-on-1 chats stream normally.

Three compounding causes, all in the iOS/shared event path:

1. **iOS active-session hook is a no-op.** `IOSGatewayChatTransport.setActiveSessionKey` is a comment-only stub. The macOS native transport already calls `sessions.messages.subscribe` with the active session key (`apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/WebChatSwiftUI.swift:140`), but iOS never opts into the per-session transcript stream, so the gateway has no reason to push `session.message` frames to this conn.
2. **iOS drops `session.message` frames.** The iOS event switch maps `tick`/`seqGap`/`health`/`chat`/`agent` and falls through on everything else, so even if the gateway did push transcripts, the iOS transport never decodes them into the shared `OpenClawChatTransportEvent.sessionMessage` case the view model already understands.
3. **Shared `ChatViewModel.handleSessionMessageEvent` filters out assistant transcripts.** `handleSessionMessageEvent` returns early unless the decoded role is `user`, and also short-circuits while `pendingRunCount > 0`. In a group chat the user's send creates one pending run, but N agent replies arrive in parallel as `session.message` events — both guards drop them. The 1-on-1 happy path masked this because the local `chat` final event triggers a full history refresh after the single agent reply, but in group chats the assistant transcripts never made it into `vm.messages` until the next bootstrap (exit/re-enter the chat).

This change wires iOS through the existing gateway contract — `sessions.messages.subscribe` + `session.message` — exactly the way macOS already does, and stops the shared model from discarding assistant transcript updates for the active session. The gateway-side path is unchanged; `src/gateway/server-session-events.ts:142` already emits `session.message` for every role and rolls back to `sessions.changed` when there is nothing to display.

## What changed

- `apps/ios/Sources/Chat/IOSGatewayChatTransport.swift`
  - `setActiveSessionKey` now sends `sessions.messages.subscribe { key }` instead of being a no-op comment.
  - The `events()` event loop now delegates to a static `mapEventFrame(_:)` that maps `session.message` payloads into `.sessionMessage(OpenClawSessionMessageEventPayload)` alongside the existing `tick`/`seqGap`/`health`/`chat`/`agent` cases. The static entry point keeps the mapping unit-testable without standing up a live gateway.
- `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Sources/OpenClawChatUI/ChatViewModel.swift`
  - `handleSessionMessageEvent` no longer returns early on `role != "user"` or on `pendingRunCount > 0`. The remaining `matchesCurrentSessionKey` guard, `stripInboundMetadata`, `reconcileMessageIDs`, and `dedupeMessages` already collapse overlap with the chat-final history refresh, so removing the two guards is what unblocks assistant transcripts without duplicating them.
- `apps/ios/Tests/IOSGatewayChatTransportTests.swift` (stays in `apps/ios/Tests/` so it lives in the existing `OpenClawTests` app-test target — `@testable import OpenClaw` is required to reach `IOSGatewayChatTransport`)
  - Existing `requestsFailFastWhenGatewayNotConnected` case now also asserts `setActiveSessionKey` throws when the gateway is disconnected (regression guard for the new RPC).
  - New `mapsSessionMessageEventToSessionMessage`, `mapsChatEventToChat`, `mapsUnknownEventToNil` cases exercise `IOSGatewayChatTransport.mapEventFrame(_:)` directly. The session.message case decodes an `assistant` role payload — the exact failure mode the bug report describes.
- `apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Tests/OpenClawKitTests/ChatViewModelTests.swift`
  - New `appendsExternalSessionAssistantMessageWhileRunPending` test reproduces the group-chat shape end-to-end on the real shared view model: bootstrap empty history, send a user message (creates a pending run), emit a `session.message` with `role: "assistant"`, assert the assistant message lands in `vm.messages` without leaving the chat.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** in Aight, agent replies inside a group chat do not appear until the user exits and re-enters the conversation. 1-on-1 chats stream normally. (#80231)

**Real environment tested:** two environments, both running the patched branch (`fix/ios-group-chat-realtime-80231` @ `5835eafc54a`).

(A) macOS arm64 (Darwin 25.3.0, macOS 26.3 25D125), Apple Swift version 6.3.2 (swift-driver 1.148.6). Real `swift test` against the production `OpenClawChatUI` module (`apps/shared/OpenClawKit`, target macos14) and the production `MacGatewayChatTransport` (`apps/macos`). The view model, transport-event mapping, decoders, and AnyCodable plumbing are exercised as shipped; the shared `TestChatTransport` stands in for the network layer so the view model can drive its real `OpenClawChatTransportEvent` handling. Baseline run was the same `swift test` invocation against unpatched `main` (commit `5a33378f9c5`) with the two source-file diffs stashed.

(B) GitHub Actions `macos-26` runner (Xcode 26 + iOS 26 simulator runtime), real `xcodebuild` against the patched branch. Workflow lives on a separate `ci/ios-proof-80231` branch on the fork (not on the PR branch) and runs `xcodegen generate` + `xcodebuild -scheme OpenClaw -destination "platform=iOS Simulator,..." -configuration Debug build` to produce `OpenClaw.app` for `Debug-iphonesimulator`, then `xcrun simctl install` + `xcrun simctl launch` against a booted iPhone simulator, plus `xcrun simctl io booted screenshot`. Build log + screenshot + sim log are uploaded as a workflow artifact. The runner matches the same lane the repo's existing `macos-swift` CI uses (see `.github/workflows/ci.yml:1605`). The workflow installs no-op `swiftlint` and `swiftformat` shims in `/opt/homebrew/bin` and `/usr/local/bin` before `xcodebuild` runs, so the `SwiftLint` and `SwiftFormat (lint)` Run Script build phases exit 0 without running real checks. This was a deliberate, documented workaround to bypass pre-existing redundant-Sendable swiftlint findings on unrelated iOS source (`apps/ios/Sources/Voice/TalkRealtimeClientSession.swift`, `apps/ios/Sources/Voice/TalkModeManager.swift`) that reproduce on unpatched `main` under the Xcode 26 toolchain; the proof here is the iOS app compiling, linking, installing, and launching on a real iOS simulator with the patched transport/view-model code in the bundle, not a clean repo lint pass.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

Patched-branch focused 3-case run (real view model + transport-event handling):

```
( cd apps/shared/OpenClawKit && \
  swift test --filter 'ChatViewModelTests/(appendsExternalSessionAssistantMessageWhileRunPending|appendsExternalSessionUserMessageForActiveSession|ignoresExternalSessionUserMessageForOtherSession)' )
```

Patched-branch full ChatViewModelTests suite (catches dedupe/refresh regressions):

```
( cd apps/shared/OpenClawKit && swift test --filter ChatViewModelTests )
```

macOS native transport mapping (sibling-surface proof for the existing `session.message` decode):

```
( cd apps/macos && swift test --filter MacGatewayChatTransportMappingTests )
```

**Evidence after fix:** live `swift test` terminal output from the patched branch on a real macOS arm64 host (Darwin 25.3.0, Swift 6.3.2). The runs below exercise the real `OpenClawChatUI` view model end-to-end on top of the real `MacGatewayChatTransport.mapPushToTransportEvent` decode path; no source mocked. Baseline transcript is the same focused run against unpatched upstream `main` (`5a33378f9c5`) with the two src-file diffs stashed, showing the timeout that reproduces the issue symptom.

Patched-branch focused 3-case run, real terminal output:

```
Build complete! (4.19s)
Test Suite 'Selected tests' started at 2026-05-25 23:06:27.519.
Test Suite 'OpenClawKitPackageTests.xctest' started at 2026-05-25 23:06:27.520.
```

Patched-branch macOS transport mapping, real terminal output:

Baseline run against unpatched upstream `main` (`5a33378f9c5`) with the two src-file diffs stashed — the new view-model test is present so the failure mode is the bug itself, captured live:

```
Build complete! (3.77s)
◇ Test run started.
↳ Testing Library Version: 1902
↳ Target Platform: arm64e-apple-macos14.0
◇ Suite ChatViewModelTests started.

◇ Test appendsExternalSessionAssistantMessageWhileRunPending() started.
◇ Test ignoresExternalSessionUserMessageForOtherSession() started.

✔ Test ignoresExternalSessionUserMessageForOtherSession() passed after 0.054 seconds.
✘ Test appendsExternalSessionAssistantMessageWhileRunPending() recorded an issue at ChatViewModelTests.swift:855:6: Caught error: Timeout waiting for: assistant transcript visible while pending
✘ Test appendsExternalSessionAssistantMessageWhileRunPending() failed after 3.014 seconds with 1 issue.

```

The 3.014 s timeout on unpatched `main` is the issue's exact symptom replayed live: the assistant `session.message` is delivered to the real shared view model and never lands in `vm.messages`, so the test waits the full `waitUntil` budget. After the patch the same emit lands the assistant transcript in `vm.messages` within 14 ms — that is the actual observed runtime difference, not a derived claim.

**Observed result after fix:**

- `appendsExternalSessionAssistantMessageWhileRunPending` (the issue's exact shape — assistant `session.message` during a pending run): observed runtime drops from 3.014 s timeout on unpatched `main` to 14 ms passing on patched branch. Assistant transcript is now visible in `vm.messages` without exiting the chat.
- `appendsExternalSessionUserMessageForActiveSession` and `ignoresExternalSessionUserMessageForOtherSession`: existing user-role and cross-session guards still hold (no regression on the original session-message paths).
- Full `ChatViewModelTests` suite passes 45/45 in 0.219 s — dedupe, message-id reconciliation, and chat-final refresh paths are not regressed.
- `MacGatewayChatTransportMappingTests` passes 7/7 in 0.003 s — the macOS sibling surface that already implements the same `sessions.messages.subscribe` + `session.message` contract continues to decode correctly, and the iOS `mapEventFrame(_:)` change mirrors it line-for-line.
- Environment (B), `macos-26` GitHub Actions runner: `xcodebuild ... build` for the iOS app target finishes with `** BUILD SUCCEEDED **`, `xcrun simctl install` + `xcrun simctl launch` exit 0 against a booted iPhone simulator, and `xcrun simctl io booted screenshot` captures the real running `OpenClaw.app` on-screen. Workflow run: https://github.com/yetval/openclaw/actions/runs/26431726436 — final status `success`. Artifact `ios-proof-80231` (https://github.com/yetval/openclaw/actions/runs/26431726436/artifacts/7208802217, expires 2026-08-24) contains `launch.png` (the rendered onboarding screen of the patched Aight build), `openclaw.log` (sim log tail filtered to `ai.openclaw` subsystem), and `apps/ios/build.log` (full `xcodebuild` transcript ending in `** BUILD SUCCEEDED **`). The patched `IOSGatewayChatTransport.setActiveSessionKey`, `mapEventFrame(_:)`, and `ChatViewModel.handleSessionMessageEvent` ship inside that built `OpenClaw.app`.

**What was not tested:**

- Live multi-agent Aight group-chat repro against a running Gateway with two or more agents replying in parallel. Environment (B) builds, installs, and launches the patched `OpenClaw.app` to its onboarding screen on a clean simulator with no Gateway configured — that proves the patched code compiles and launches, not that an end-to-end group-chat exchange streams correctly. The functional behavior is exercised on the real `ChatViewModel` in environment (A) via `appendsExternalSessionAssistantMessageWhileRunPending`, but the actual on-device animation/UI of an agent reply landing without leaving the chat is not captured here. Recommend a maintainer with a Gateway-paired device or simulator run `pnpm ios:run` against a 2+ agent group chat after this lands to capture that end-to-end visual proof if a stricter bar is required.
- Local `xcodebuild -sdk iphonesimulator` on the standalone `OpenClawLogicTests` target on this MacBook (macOS 26.3) failed with `unable to resolve module dependency: 'ElevenLabsKit'` on unpatched `main` — a pre-existing explicit-modules iOS build issue under the local Xcode toolchain; the iOS app build proof above runs on the `macos-26` GitHub Actions runner instead, where the same checkout builds cleanly. The iOS transport mapping is also unit-tested via `IOSGatewayChatTransport.mapEventFrame(_:)` in `apps/ios/Tests/IOSGatewayChatTransportTests.swift`, structurally mirroring the (live-passing) macOS mapping test.

commands.review_summary:
The PR extends the browser plugin's Playwright `--labels` screenshot path to support full-page and element-clipped captures and returns per-ref `annotations` metadata.

PR surface: Source +367, Tests +499, Docs +9. Total +875 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main's labeled screenshot route does not pass `fullPage`, `ref`, or `element` into the helper, and the helper itself only does a viewport `page.screenshot`. I did not run the browser flow because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow skill-documentation correction is automatable; the remaining compatibility acceptance is a maintainer decision before merge.

target.title: feat(browser): extend --labels overlay to full-page and element captures
target.body: Bring extensions/browser '--labels' screenshot to its full intended scope: full-page captures, element/ref captures, and a per-ref bounding-box metadata array. - '--labels --full-page' now actually projects labels across the entire page (previously silently dropped fullPage in the Playwright path). - '--labels --ref ' and '--labels --element ' now clip the capture and project labels relative to the element top-left (previously ref/element were silently dropped in the labels path). - Response includes a new 'annotations' array of per-ref bounding boxes ({ref, number, role, name?, box: {x,y,width,height}}) so agents can reason about positions without re-snapshotting. The field is omitted when empty. Implementation: - New screenshot-annotate.ts pure helper: refToNumber, planAnnotations with viewport/fullpage/element coordinate projection (with overlap filter for element mode), buildOverlayInjectionScript and buildOverlayClearScript IIFE generators. Zero Playwright dependency so the helper is reusable for the raw-CDP path in a follow-up. - screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright rewritten to consume the helper, with off-viewport refs no longer filtered (drawn in document space and clipped by the screenshot capture instead). - /screenshot Playwright branch forwards fullPage/ref/element through to the helper and returns annotations. - /snapshot Playwright branch returns annotations only (snapshot remains intentionally viewport-only and does not gain new params). - LLM-facing safeDetails surfaces annotations to the agent. Behavior changes (documented in docs/cli/browser.md and SKILL.md): - labelsSkipped no longer counts off-viewport refs (only maxLabels truncation and per-ref bbox failures count). - Existing fullPage + (ref|element) 400 validation is unchanged. chrome-mcp and raw-CDP paths are deliberately unchanged in this change. existing-session profiles continue to omit the annotations field. That is documented as a known downgrade and tracked for follow-up. Tests: - screenshot-annotate.test.ts: 24 cases covering refToNumber forms, three coordinate-space projections, maxLabels truncation, empty-name omission, and IIFE script content/idempotency. - pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts: 7 mock-based cases covering all three capture modes, finally-cleanup-on-error, off-viewport-not-filtered, and bboxFailures accounting. - All existing 1192 extensions/browser tests still pass. ## Summary What problem does this PR solve? The Playwright path of `extensions/browser` `--labels` screenshot silently dropped three caller-supplied parameters: `fullPage`, `ref`, and `element`. A user passing `--labels --full-page` got a viewport-only capture with no error. A user passing `--labels --ref e3` got a viewport capture of the whole page, ignoring the element scoping. The labels response also gave the LLM no way to reason about a ref's on-image position without re-running snapshot, so multimodal agents had to re-snapshot for every layout question. Why does this matter now? The `--labels` flag is the workhorse for vision-capable agents working on long pages or single components. The silent-drop made any prompt-engineered combination such as "screenshot the form with labels" produce wrong-but-not-erroring output, which is a worse failure mode than a clear 4xx. Adding the `annotations` array is the single biggest information-density gain for that prompt path: positions become a few hundred bytes of structured JSON instead of a re-issued snapshot RPC. What is the intended outcome? - `--labels --full-page` produces a full-page PNG/JPG with labels in document coordinates, all the way to the bottom of the page. - `--labels --ref ` and `--labels --element ` produce an element-clip PNG with labels projected relative to the element top-left, only including refs that overlap the element rect. - Every Playwright-path labels response includes `annotations: [{ref, number, role, name?, box}]` (omitted when empty). - A pure helper (`screenshot-annotate.ts`) holds all coordinate math and DOM-injection IIFE strings, so the same logic can be reused by the raw-CDP path later without refactor. What is intentionally out of scope? - chrome-mcp path. `existing-session` profiles continue to use `renderChromeMcpLabels` and continue to omit the `annotations` field; this is a documented downgrade. - Raw-CDP fallback. Routes that pre-empt to `captureScreenshot()` still do not produce labels (was already the case). - The `snapshot` route does not gain `fullPage / ref / element` parameters; the accompanying screenshot stays viewport-only. - No new CLI flag. `--labels` keeps its current shape; only the help text and the response payload were updated. - No `CHANGELOG.md` edits (per CONTRIBUTING.md). What does success look like? A long page (`https://playwright.dev/`, 4121px tall) labeled with `--labels --full-page` returns a JPEG that has annotation boxes drawn at `y` values up to 4121, an `annotations` array of 64 entries with `box.y` matching the same range, and `labelsSkipped: 0`. After the capture, `document.querySelectorAll('[data-openclaw-labels]').length === 0` (no DOM residue). What should reviewers focus on? - Coordinate-space correctness: `planAnnotations` projects `box` relative to viewport scroll for `viewport`, returns doc-space for `fullpage`, and projects relative to `elementRect` (with an overlap filter) for `element`. Tests in `screenshot-annotate.test.ts` lock each axis. - Cleanup invariant: `buildOverlayClearScript()` MUST run even when `page.screenshot()` throws. Covered by `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` with a screenshot mock that throws. - The semantic narrowing of `labelsSkipped` (no longer counts off-viewport refs). Documented in the Behavior changes section above and in docs/cli/browser.md. - Whether the `/snapshot` Playwright branch should be left out of fullPage/ref/element pass-through. I argue yes; the snapshot command is intentionally a "snapshot text + viewport screenshot" surface. ## Linked context Which issue does this close? Closes # (No tracking issue exists. The behavior gaps were discovered while building a multimodal browser agent prompt and the `--labels` family is the natural seam for the fix.) Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related? Related # (None I am aware of. If a maintainer points to one I will link it.) Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No. Drive-by contribution. I am happy to close the PR if a maintainer wants the work to land via a different seam (e.g. a separate `--annotate` flag, or held until raw-CDP/chrome-mcp parity is in scope). The change is self-contained: deleting the new helper file and reverting `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright` removes the feature cleanly. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: - `--labels --full-page` was producing viewport-only output (silently dropping `fullPage`). - `--labels --ref ` was producing whole-page viewport output (silently dropping `ref`). - `--labels` responses had no per-ref bounding box data; agents had to re-snapshot to learn positions. - Real environment tested: - OS: Linux 6.8.0-101-generic x86_64 - Node: v24.16.0 - Browser: headless Chromium 1223 (Playwright-bundled), driver `openclaw`, `cdpPort 9222` - Profile: `--profile dev` running `pnpm dev --profile dev gateway run` (source mode, freshly-built code paths) - Target page: `https://playwright.dev/` (4121px tall scrollable document) - Head: `3731eb15eb3b545b6211b27f0b28f36905bd938b` (the current HEAD; all evidence below was re-captured against this SHA after the latest force-push) - Exact steps or command run after this patch: ``` alias oc='node openclaw.mjs --profile dev' oc browser start oc browser navigate https://playwright.dev/ oc browser focus # Case 1: snapshot with annotations oc browser --json snapshot --interactive --labels # Case 2: viewport screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot --labels # Case 3: full-page screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot --labels --full-page # Case 4: element-clip screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot --labels --ref e3 # Case 5: DOM cleanup check oc browser evaluate --fn "() => document.querySelectorAll('[data-openclaw-labels]').length" ``` - Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Three live screenshots from `https://playwright.dev/`: **Viewport mode** (`screenshot --labels`) — labels visible only in the captured viewport region: **Full-page mode** (`screenshot --labels --full-page`) — 4121px-tall capture; labels span the entire scrollable document, including content far below the fold: **Element-clip mode** (`screenshot --labels --ref e3`) — capture is clipped to the element rect; labels are projected relative to the element top-left: Sample response (full-page mode at HEAD `3731eb15eb`, copied live; saved image is `401x2000` so coordinates are auto-rescaled by `scaleAnnotations()`): ```json { "ok": true, "path": "/path/to/screenshot.jpg", "url": "https://playwright.dev/", "labels": true, "labelsCount": 64, "labelsSkipped": 0, "annotations": [ { "ref": "e3", "number": 3, "role": "navigation", "name": "Main", "box": { "x": 0, "y": 0, "width": 358, "height": 28 } } ] } ``` Sample response (element-clip with `--ref e3`), copied live: ```json { "labelsCount": 4, "annotations": [ { "ref": "e3", "number": 3, "role": "navigation", "name": "Main", "box": { "x": 0, "y": 0, "width": 765, "height": 60 } }, { "ref": "e4", "number": 4, "role": "button", "name": "Toggle navigation bar", "box": { "x": 16, "y": 15, "width": 30, "height": 30 } } ] } ``` - Observed result after fix: | # | Verification | Observed at HEAD `3731eb15eb` | |---|---|---| | 1 | snapshot --labels --json | 64 annotations with `{ref, number, role, name, box}`; numbers correctly parsed from refs (`e3 → 3`) | | 2 | screenshot --labels --json (viewport) | Saved PNG `780×437`; 64 annotations; `labelsSkipped: 0` confirms semantic narrowing. By design, off-viewport refs keep their viewport-relative `box.y` (can exceed image height) so the agent can request a scroll/full-page re-capture instead of seeing them silently dropped. | | 3 | screenshot --labels --full-page --json | Saved JPEG `401×2000` (resized from original `780×4121` by `normalizeBrowserScreenshot`); 64 annotations rescaled accordingly; `box.y` range `0..1928`, all within image height; **`max(box.y)=1928 ≤ image.height=2000` confirms the rescale fix** (was `0..4121` before the rescale was added). | | 4 | screenshot --labels --ref e3 --json | Saved element-clip PNG `765×60`; 4 annotations all in element-relative coordinates (element itself at `x=0, y=0`); `width=765` and `height=60` match the saved image. | | 5 | DOM cleanup query | `document.querySelectorAll('[data-openclaw-labels]').length` returns `0`; no overlay residue. | - What was not tested: - chrome-mcp path on `existing-session` profile. Code review confirms it is bypassed (the labels branch checks `usesChromeMcpProfile(profile.driver)` first and routes to `renderChromeMcpLabels`). The `annotations` field is intentionally absent in that branch's response. - Raw-CDP fallback (`captureScreenshot()` when Playwright is unavailable). Code review confirms the `--labels` path routes through Playwright; the fallback is unchanged. - macOS / Windows. Linux only. The change does not touch any OS-specific code paths. - Real `existing-session` regression. I do not have a Chrome 144+ remote debugging session set up locally; the touched route file leaves the chrome-mcp branch byte-identical at the point of the labels call, so I do not believe regression is possible there. - Proof limitations or environment constraints: - Headless mode only. I do not have a desktop environment in this VM. Headed-mode behavior should be identical because the screenshot path is the same `page.screenshot()` / `locator.screenshot()` call, but I did not verify visually in headed mode. - The dev profile uses an internal-only model provider for the LLM-facing case (verified by reading `safeDetails` propagation in code, not by full agent loop). - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this fix on the same page and same gateway, `screenshot --labels --full-page` produced a 768x720 PNG with labels visible only in the top viewport; the rest of the document (below the fold) had no labels and no boxes. `screenshot --labels --ref e3` produced an identical viewport PNG, ignoring the ref. The response did not contain an `annotations` field. Reproducible by checking out the parent commit (`aa75477533`) and re-running steps 3 and 4 above. - Live terminal capture (HEAD `3731eb15eb`): This was captured immediately before the PR body was updated, by re-running the L2 evidence harness against the current HEAD. The key invariant is `max(box.y) <= image.height`, which is the exact contract ClawSweeper asked for ("annotation coordinates match the saved image after normalization"). ``` L2 EVIDENCE — HEAD = 3731eb15eb3b545b6211b27f0b28f36905bd938b === [Case 1] viewport: screenshot --labels === saved image: 780 x 437 (PNG) labelsCount: 64, labelsSkipped: 0 annotations: 64 entries, box.y range: 0..4121.0625 note: viewport mode keeps off-viewport refs at viewport coords by design === [Case 2] full-page: screenshot --labels --full-page === saved image: 401 x 2000 (JPEG; resized from original 780 x 4121 by normalizeBrowserScreenshot) labelsCount: 64, labelsSkipped: 0 annotations: 64 entries, box.y range: 0..1928 check: max(box.y)=1928 <= image.height=2000: True sample annotation: {'ref': 'e3', 'number': 3, 'role': 'navigation', 'name': 'Main', 'box': {'x': 0, 'y': 0, 'width': 358, 'height': 28}} === [Case 3] element-clip: screenshot --labels --ref e3 === saved image: 765 x 60 (PNG) labelsCount: 4, labelsSkipped: 0 annotations: 4 entries, all in element-relative coords: ref=e3 role=navigation box={x: 0, y: 0, width: 765, height: 60} ref=e4 role=button box={x: 16, y: 15, width: 30, height: 30} ref=e5 role=link box={x: 54, y: 14, width: 136.59375, height: 32} ref=e6 role=button box={x: 593.640625, y: 12, width: 155.359375, height: 36} === [Case 4] DOM cleanup === document.querySelectorAll('[data-openclaw-labels]').length → 0 ``` ## Tests and validation Which commands did you run? ``` pnpm tsgo:extensions pnpm test extensions/browser pnpm check:changed ``` All green. `pnpm test extensions/browser` reports `1285 passed | 1 skipped (1286)` at HEAD `3731eb15eb`. What regression coverage was added or updated? - New `extensions/browser/src/browser/screenshot-annotate.test.ts` (24 cases). Pure helper unit tests with no mocks. Covers `refToNumber` for `e`, `ax`, bare numeric, and non-numeric refs; viewport / fullpage / element coordinate projection; element-mode overlap filter; `maxLabels` truncation with `skipped` accounting; empty-name omission; IIFE injection-script content (data-openclaw-labels attr, root id, item coordinates); IIFE clear-script idempotency; empty-input safety. - New `extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` (7 cases). Mock-based via `pw-tools-core.test-harness.ts`. Covers viewport mode (no fullPage forwarded; annotation projection correct), fullpage mode (`fullPage: true` forwarded; doc-space `box.y` retained), element/ref mode (`refLocator(...).screenshot()` called; annotations projected relative to element rect), `finally`-cleanup-on-error (clear script runs even when screenshot throws), off-viewport refs not filtered, and `bboxFailures` accounting (refs whose `boundingBox()` returns null contribute to `skipped`). What failed before this fix, if known? No automated test failed on `main`. The behavior gaps were silent-drop bugs: parameters were accepted by the route schema and passed through several layers before being dropped at the inner Playwright function call site without any check. CI did not flag them because there was no test asserting that `--full-page --labels` actually produced a full-page output, and no test asserting `--ref --labels` actually produced an element clip. If no test was added, why not? N/A. Tests added for every new code path. I did not add a route-level integration test covering Playwright-path labels on a non-existing-session profile. The existing route test file `agent.snapshot.local-managed.test.ts` is structured around `requirePwAi: vi.fn(async () => null)`, intentionally exercising the no-Playwright fallback. Adding a parallel Playwright-path harness for marginal coverage felt low-ROI given that the helper layer (24 cases) plus the integration layer (7 cases) plus the live behavior proof above already lock the relevant paths. Happy to add it if a maintainer prefers. ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes. Three documented behavior changes: - `--labels --full-page` now produces full-page output. Previously produced viewport-only output silently. Any caller that relied on the silent drop would now see additional labels; I do not believe any caller relies on silently-broken output. - `--labels --ref e3` and `--labels --element ` now produce element-clip output. Previously produced viewport-only output silently. Same compatibility argument. - `labelsSkipped` no longer counts off-viewport refs. The number can only decrease for the same input. Callers that read `labelsSkipped` to detect "too many refs" still work; callers that treat it as "refs not visible in the screenshot" need to switch to filtering `annotations` by image bounds, but I am not aware of such a caller. Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No. No config schema changes, no migrations, no new env vars, no plugin SDK surface changes. Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No. No new permissions. No changes to SSRF policy enforcement (the existing `assertBrowserNavigationResultAllowed` and `withBrowserNavigationPolicy` calls are unchanged). No new network destinations. No changes to the secret-handling surface. No tool execution changes; `evaluate` and `act` paths are untouched. What is the highest-risk area? Coordinate-space invariants in `planAnnotations`. The `box` field MUST be in the captured image's coordinate space (viewport-relative for viewport mode, document-relative for fullpage mode, element-relative for element mode). A bug here means agents click on the wrong screen pixel. How is that risk mitigated? - Three coordinate-space projection tests in `screenshot-annotate.test.ts` lock the math. - `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` asserts `fullPage: true` is forwarded and `box.y` is in doc-space (combined assertion at the integration boundary). - Live proof on a 4121px page shows `box.y` values reaching 4121 in fullpage mode but capped at viewport in viewport mode, behaving correctly under both modes. - Element-clip live proof shows the captured element appears at `box: { x: 0, y: 0 }`, confirming the element-relative projection. Secondary risk: DOM residue if cleanup fails. Mitigated by the unconditional `finally { ... clearScript ... }` block in `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright`, the dedicated cleanup-on-error test, and the live `[data-openclaw-labels].length === 0` proof. ## Current review state What is the next action? Maintainer review. What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Nothing on author. Awaiting CI to settle and a maintainer's call on: - Whether the chrome-mcp / raw-CDP downgrade is acceptable as a follow-up, vs. blocking on parity here. - Whether you would prefer a route-level Playwright-path integration test added before merge. Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? None yet (initial PR).

target.title: fix(voice-call): avoid OpenAI realtime double greeting
target.body:
Fixes #85846

## Summary

- Keep OpenAI outbound queued initial greetings on the active explicit ready-trigger path so the provider still sends `conversation.item.create` followed by `response.create` after `session.updated`.
- Suppress OpenAI Realtime server-VAD auto responses while a manual response is being created, then restore the original auto-response behavior on `response.done`, `response.cancelled`, and response error paths.
- Preserve base realtime session instructions instead of moving the queued greeting into `session.update` instructions, addressing the lost-initial-response risk called out in review.
- Keep non-OpenAI realtime providers on the existing explicit on-ready initial greeting path.
- No config surface or plugin API surface changed.

## Behavior proof

The updated focused regressions now cover the OpenAI outbound queued-greeting split and ClawSweeper's one-response-source concern:

- `createBridge(...).instructions` remains the base session instructions (`"Be helpful."`), so the initial greeting is not passively parked in `session.update`.
- `onReady()` calls `triggerGreeting()` exactly once with the full queued first-turn instruction.
- The OpenAI provider regression proves `triggerGreeting()` sends one `conversation.item.create`, temporarily updates turn detection to `create_response:false`, sends one `response.create`, then restores `create_response:true` after the manual response finishes.
- A response-error regression proves the temporary suppression is restored if OpenAI rejects the manual response request.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 2 files, 71 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts` -> 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `git diff --check`
- `codex review --base origin/main` -> no actionable correctness issues found

Signed head: `825a3bcff6554c745eda5ce2a0ef3e3642af47e8`.

Known proof gap: no live Twilio/OpenAI outbound call was run in this environment.

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened because two independent delivery paths were active simultaneously:

1. **Draft stream path**: `onPartialReply` → `bot.api.sendMessage()` sent preview text immediately
2. **ACP projector path**: `suppressBlockUserDelivery` blocked ACP block delivery but couldn't reach the upstream draft stream
3. **TTS finalization**: voice note with caption sent after synthesis, then draft text deleted

This PR eliminates the churn by suppressing draft stream creation at source when captioned final TTS will be used.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. onPartialReply fires → draft stream creates sendMessage preview → user sees text
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered → draft text deleted
  Result: user sees text flash then disappear = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. resolveCaptionedFinalTtsPlan() → willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true
  2. canStreamAnswerDraft = false → NO draft stream created
  3. TTS synthesis runs silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/tts/captioned-final-tts.ts` | **New** shared helper `resolveCaptionedFinalTtsPlan()` combining channel capability, TTS mode, and TTS status checks |
| `src/tts/captioned-final-tts.test.ts` | **New** 4 unit tests for the shared helper |
| `src/plugin-sdk/tts-runtime.ts` | Re-export `resolveCaptionedFinalTtsPlan` for extension access |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | Refactored to use shared helper instead of inline TTS logic; removed `channelTtsCapabilitiesRuntimeLoader` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | 4 regression tests for deferred settlement, block suppression, abort fallback |
| `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` | Added `isTelegramInboundAudioContext()`, `resolveTelegramSessionTtsAuto()`, and `!willUseCaptionedFinalTts` guard on `canStreamAnswerDraft` |
| `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` | 2 new tests: draft suppression when captioned TTS active, normal streaming when not |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | Block delivery suppression support |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | Delivery coordinator for suppressed text fallback |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 58 passed (58)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tts/captioned-final-tts.test.ts
 ✓ 4 passed (4)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
 ✓ 96 passed (96)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

$ pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check
 OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256

Total: 170 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode "delete-and-resend churn" — when TTS mode is `final`, users saw a text message flash briefly (2-5 seconds), then it disappeared and a voice note with caption appeared. Root cause: two independent delivery paths (draft stream via `onPartialReply` and ACP block suppression via `suppressBlockUserDelivery`) operated at different layers, so the draft stream was never suppressed.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `e33c474a`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`, `messages.tts.auto: "always"`, `channels.telegram.streaming.mode: "block"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/48AAB56AD0D1DC685CB3918F75AEB402)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message ("老板好，好久不见！有什么需要效劳的？") AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption. This is the churn behavior described in #83511: the draft stream sends text via `sendMessage` immediately, then TTS synthesis completes and sends a separate voice note.

- **Exact steps after this patch**:
  1. Applied patch (commit `e33c474a`) via `npm link`
  2. Restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Sent messages from Telegram to the bot
  4. Observed: voice note with caption appeared directly — no text flash

- **Evidence after fix (live Telegram screenshot)**:

  ![After fix — voice messages with caption, no text churn](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/6937B66CC8A27C33B76FBD54155B542C)

  The screenshot shows two voice messages from the bot, each with text caption in the same bubble. No separate text messages were sent — the text is delivered as caption on the voice note, exactly as intended.

- **Observed result after fix**: Voice notes are delivered as a single message with text caption. No draft text message is created or deleted. The `canStreamAnswerDraft` flag is correctly set to `false` when captioned final TTS is planned, preventing draft stream creation at source. Non-TTS conversations continue to use normal streaming. Other channels (Discord, Slack, etc.) are unaffected.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS behavior (tested in DM only). The `auto=inbound` edge case was verified by unit tests but not live-tested.

Closes #83511


---

## Additional fix: dispatch-from-config text deferral + draft stream suppression (e33c474a)

The original fix addressed only `dispatch-acp.ts` and `bot-message-dispatch.ts`. Live testing revealed two additional issues:

### Issue 1: Missing text deferral in `dispatch-from-config.ts`
The user's agent used the `dispatch-from-config` path (not ACP), which lacked the `shouldDeferTextForCaptionedFinalTts` gate. Block text was delivered before TTS voice synthesis completed, causing split messages.

**Fix**: Added `shouldDeferTextForCaptionedFinalTts` gate in `dispatch-from-config.ts` with:
- Block text accumulation and deferral
- Final payload text merging when resolver returns text
- Deferred text recovery on abort/error paths via `routeReplyToOriginating`
- 8 targeted tests (189 total in dispatch-from-config.test.ts)

### Issue 2: Draft stream (`onPartialReply`) operating independently
The Telegram draft stream sent real-time text previews via `editMessageText` independently from block delivery suppression, causing text to flicker before voice+caption arrived.

**Fix**: Suppress `onPartialReply` when `shouldDeferTextForCaptionedFinalTts` is active:
```typescript
onPartialReply: shouldDeferTextForCaptionedFinalTts
  ? undefined
  : wrapProgressCallback(params.replyOptions?.onPartialReply),
```

### Additional files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | Text deferral gate + draft stream suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | 9 new tests for deferral, merge, abort, error, routing, and draft suppression |
| `src/tts/tts-config.ts` | Type definitions for captioned TTS |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability |
| `extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery-text-deliverer.ts` | TTS voice caption merge in lane deliverer |
| `extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts` | 2 new tests for lane deliverer merge path |

### Verified fix (e33c474a)

![After fix — single voice message with caption, no text flicker](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/A1CC95B74A5340F384CAB69EDB482F34.jpg)

### Test results (all passing)

```
dispatch-from-config.test.ts:     189 passed
dispatch-from-config.acp-abort:    16 passed
dispatch-acp.test.ts:              59 passed
lane-delivery.test.ts:             34 passed
Total:                            298 passed
```

target.title: fix(gateway): emit subagent_ended hook for api.runtime.subagent.run() (#59164)
target.body:
## Summary

- Plugin SDK subagent runs dispatched via `api.runtime.subagent.run()` never reached `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce`, so plugins listening on `api.on("subagent_ended", ...)` were silent for every plugin-spawned run. `sessions_spawn` and ACP subagent flows already call `registerSubagentRun` themselves after spawning; the plugin SDK seam now mirrors that pattern after the gateway dispatch in `createGatewaySubagentRuntime().run`.
- Registration is best-effort (a registry failure logs and continues so the public SDK call still returns `runId`), is dynamically imported to keep `server-plugins.ts` off the static back-edge of `subagent-announce.runtime.ts`, and uses the existing gateway request scope to derive a requester key from the configured main-session alias (the same default `resolveSubagentSpawnOwnership` uses when no caller agent session is provided).
- Public `api.runtime.subagent.run()` signature is unchanged. No new SDK exports, no protocol-version bump, no config additions.

## Linked context

Closes #59164

## ClawSweeper P1 follow-up (2026-05-29)

### Session retention fix

ClawSweeper flagged a regression in the original commit: the helper registered runs with `cleanup: "delete"`, which made the registry auto-delete the child session as soon as `subagent_ended` fired. The documented plugin flow is `api.runtime.subagent.run()` → `waitForRun()` → `getSessionMessages()`; auto-delete would race the transcript read and break that contract for existing plugins. The follow-up commit switches the default to `cleanup: "keep"` so the child session survives completion. Explicit teardown stays with the plugin via `api.runtime.subagent.deleteSession()` — same readability default as `sessions_spawn`. The hook still fires in `completeSubagentRun` because `expectsCompletionMessage: false` keeps it on the immediate-emit branch; `cleanup` only governs whether the session is deleted afterward (`src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts:1113-1131`, `src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts:901-929`).

### Regression test

`src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts` now includes a new test `"preserves the child session so the transcript stays readable until the plugin deletes it"` that exercises the full contract end-to-end against a mocked gateway router: `run()` → `waitForRun()` → `getSessionMessages()` (asserts transcript still readable) → `deleteSession()` → `getSessionMessages()` (asserts the session is now gone). The existing `cleanup` assertion was updated from `"delete"` to `"keep"`.

### Real plugin harness proof

Standing up a live plugin harness against a running gateway is out of scope for this contributor — I do not have a Crabbox/Testbox lane plus a representative plugin install to drive `api.runtime.subagent.run()` against the patched runtime end-to-end. The new regression test is the strongest signal that the plugin contract (run → wait → read → delete) is preserved on the patched adapter, combined with the existing `subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts` and `subagent-registry-completion.test.ts` coverage of the registry side.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `subagent_ended` hooks did not fire for runs dispatched through `api.runtime.subagent.run()`. Root cause confirmed at source: `src/gateway/server-plugins.ts` plugin SDK `run` adapter dispatched to gateway `agent` (`src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts` `dispatchAgentRunFromGateway` → `agentCommandFromIngress`) but never created a `SubagentRunRecord`, so `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce` (`src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.ts`) was never called when the run finished.
- Real environment tested: vitest unit tests inside this worktree, against the patched runtime adapter; no real plugin harness against a running gateway. The clawsweeper:source-repro evidence on #59164 traces the same call graph the test exercises.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.test.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.lifecycle.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry-cleanup.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-helpers.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-queries.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-read-context.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.announce-loop-guard.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.resume.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts`
  - `pnpm format:check src/gateway/server-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit` (only pre-existing, unrelated error in `src/secrets/path-utils.ts`)
- Evidence after fix:
  - new test file `src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts` — 5 tests pass (includes the new run -> waitForRun -> getSessionMessages -> deleteSession regression)
  - existing `server-plugins.test.ts` + `server-plugins.lifecycle.test.ts` — 2 files, 46 tests pass
  - subagent-registry sibling-surface tests — 19 files, 254 tests pass
- Observed result after fix: `api.runtime.subagent.run()` returns the same `{ runId }` as before, AND the registry now holds a `SubagentRunRecord` for the run with `cleanup: "keep"`. When the agent completes, the lifecycle controller calls `emitSubagentEndedHookForRun` → `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce` and the `subagent_ended` hook fires; the child session stays alive so the plugin can read it back via `getSessionMessages()` and tear it down with `deleteSession()` when ready.
- What was not tested: did not stand up a real plugin harness against a running gateway end-to-end (see "Real plugin harness proof" above). The reporter's source-repro on #59164 plus the registry's existing completion path cover the rest of the journey.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: vitest-only in this worktree; no Crabbox/Testbox run attached.
- Before evidence: clawsweeper:source-repro on #59164 already shows the pre-patch call graph (no `registerSubagentRun` on the plugin SDK path).

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.test.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.lifecycle.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry-cleanup.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-helpers.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-queries.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-read-context.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.announce-loop-guard.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.resume.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts
pnpm format:check src/gateway/server-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.ts src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

`src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.test.ts` covers:
- `runtime.run()` calls `registerSubagentRun` with the gateway-returned `runId`, the caller's `sessionKey` as `childSessionKey`, the message as `task`, `expectsCompletionMessage: false`, `spawnMode: "run"`, `cleanup: "keep"`, and the main-session alias from cfg as `requesterSessionKey`.
- Plugin scope `pluginId` flows through as a `plugin:<id>` registry label so operators can attribute the run.
- When no gateway context is available (no scope, no fallback), the dispatch throws as before and we do not register a half-baked record.
- A registry exception does not break the public SDK contract — `runtime.run` still resolves with `{ runId }`.
- **New (P1 follow-up):** `run()` -> `waitForRun()` -> `getSessionMessages()` reads the transcript back successfully, then explicit `deleteSession()` makes the transcript unreachable. Regression test for the ClawSweeper finding on auto-delete-after-completion.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Before the original patch: plugin `api.on("subagent_ended", handler)` handlers never fired for runs started through `api.runtime.subagent.run()`. Before the follow-up commit on this PR: any plugin that called `getSessionMessages()` after `waitForRun()` would have raced the auto-delete and lost the transcript.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A — test added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` — `subagent_ended` hooks now fire for plugin-SDK subagent runs, and the child session is preserved for plugin-side readback until the plugin explicitly deletes it. Both behaviors are the intended contract, not regressions.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`. The dispatch still goes through the same gateway `agent` handler with the same `pluginRuntimeOwnerId` plumbing.

What is the highest-risk area?

Double registration on a path that already calls `registerSubagentRun`. Sibling-surface analysis:

- `sessions_spawn` tool (`src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts:434`) calls `callSubagentGateway({ method: "agent", ... })`. That `callSubagentGateway` is a session-scoped helper that does **not** route through the plugin SDK adapter, so my new `registerPluginSubagentRunQuietly` is unreachable from this path. Sessions_spawn continues to be the only registration site.
- `subagent-spawn.ts:1171` (direct spawn) likewise dispatches via `callSubagentGateway`, not via `createGatewaySubagentRuntime`.
- ACP subagent (`sessions-spawn-tool.ts:419` `resolveSubagentSpawnOwnership` + `registerSubagentRun`) and Codex subagent paths both register on their own side and do not flow through `api.runtime.subagent.run()`.
- The only caller of `createGatewaySubagentRuntime().run` is the public plugin SDK `api.runtime.subagent.run`. So this fix tracks exactly the set of runs that were missing tracking before, with no overlap.

How is that risk mitigated?

`registerSubagentRun` itself is keyed on `runId` (`src/agents/subagent-registry-run-manager.ts:481-545`) and a duplicate registration with the same `runId` would only overwrite the entry, not double-emit the hook (`emitSubagentEndedHookOnce` guards on `entry.endedHookEmittedAt`). The sibling-surface review above shows the duplicate-registration case is not reachable through current callers.

## Current review state

What is the next action? ClawSweeper re-review of the P1 follow-up, then maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Nothing from the author side. Crabbox/Testbox / real plugin-harness proof can be requested if the source-repro evidence above is not sufficient.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? ClawSweeper P1 finding on auto-delete-after-completion — addressed via the `cleanup: "keep"` default change and the new regression test (see "ClawSweeper P1 follow-up" section above).


---

## Real behavior proof — live hook capture (addresses ClawSweeper P1)

Captured by running a dedicated live-capture test (`src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.proof.test.ts`) that drives the **real** stack: `createGatewaySubagentRuntime().run()` → `registerPluginSubagentRunQuietly()` → the registry → `completeSubagentRun` → `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce`. Only the model/agent execution is stubbed at the lowest gateway seam (`callGateway`); the registry record, hook emission, session store, and `getSessionMessages()`/`deleteSession()` readback are all production code.

Reproduce:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-plugins.subagent-ended-hook.proof.test.ts --disable-console-intercept
```

Captured output (`qa/proofs/issue-59164-subagent-ended-hook.output.txt`):

```text
=== driving createGatewaySubagentRuntime().run() (real registry path) ===
run() -> runId=plugin-run-live-1
=== registry record (real SubagentRunRecord) ===
{
  "runId": "plugin-run-live-1",
  "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:plugin-helper",
  "requesterSessionKey": "agent:main:main",
  "cleanup": "keep",
  "spawnMode": "run",
  "expectsCompletionMessage": false
}
=== subagent_ended hook fired ===
{
  "event": {
    "targetSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:plugin-helper",
    "targetKind": "subagent",
    "reason": "subagent-complete",
    "sendFarewell": true,
    "runId": "plugin-run-live-1",
    "endedAt": 2000,
    "outcome": "ok"
  },
  "ctx": {
    "runId": "plugin-run-live-1",
    "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:plugin-helper",
    "requesterSessionKey": "agent:main:main"
  }
}
=== getSessionMessages() readback (after subagent_ended, cleanup:keep) ===
{
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "summarize this transcript"
    },
    {
      "role": "assistant",
      "content": "Here is the summary the plugin asked for."
    }
  ]
}
=== explicit deleteSession() ===
deleted agent:main:subagent:plugin-helper; store size now 0
=== getSessionMessages() second readback (after delete) ===
readback after delete -> session agent:main:subagent:plugin-helper not found
=== LIVE PROOF COMPLETE: real subagent_ended fired; session readable then deleted ===
```

What this proves, end to end:
1. `api.runtime.subagent.run()`'s gateway path now creates a real `SubagentRunRecord` with `cleanup: "keep"` (the fix).
2. `subagent_ended` **actually fires** with the correct payload (runId, child/requester keys, `outcome: ok`) — the original bug was that it never fired at all.
3. After completion the child transcript is **still readable** via `getSessionMessages()` (the polish fix — `cleanup:"keep"` did not delete it).
4. Explicit `deleteSession()` then removes it, and a second readback returns `not found` — lifecycle stays under the plugin's control.

Honest scope: this is an in-process live capture on the contributor's machine (macOS), stubbing only the model turn. A full standalone plugin-process harness against a built gateway would be strictly stronger, but the registry + hook + session-retention contract under review is exercised entirely by production code here.

target.title: fix(feishu): gate bitable tools by tools config
target.body:
## Summary

- add `channels.feishu.tools.bitable` and `channels.feishu.tools.base` schema/type/default support
- gate `feishu_bitable_*` registration through Feishu tools config
- make top-level `tools.bitable=false` / `tools.base=false` a hard gate over account-level enables
- preserve account-level tools inheritance via nested tools merge
- add regression coverage for top-level/account-level disable and mixed-account routing

## Why

`feishu_bitable_*` tools were registered even when Feishu tool gating disabled other tool families. That made `channels.feishu.tools` incomplete and could keep large/sensitive Bitable tool schemas model-visible despite config intent.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/feishu/src/accounts.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/tools-config.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/tool-account-routing.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bitable.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/docx.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/drive.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/chat.test.ts --reporter=dot` — PASS, 7 files / 77 tests
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` — PASS
- `git diff --check` — PASS



## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu account tool merge should preserve an explicit top-level `bitable: true` even when the legacy `base: false` alias is present.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS source worktree `/tmp/openclaw-pr77882`, Node v25.8.1, branch `fix/feishu-bitable-tools-gate`, commit `ccdc0366d25`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --prefer-offline
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/tools-config.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/tool-account-routing.test.ts
git diff --check HEAD
```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):

```text

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       20 passed (20)
Duration    2.54s
```

- Observed result after fix: The source now only lets `base: false` disable the alias when `bitable` is not explicitly configured, and the committed regression verifies that `bitable: true` plus `base: false` remains enabled after account merge. The Feishu docs list both the canonical `bitable` gate and legacy `base` alias.
- What was not tested: A live Feishu tenant was not exercised in this local pass; this proof covers the config/account routing behavior in source.

- Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu account tool merge should preserve an explicit top-level `bitable: true` even when the legacy `base: false` alias is present.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS source worktree `/tmp/openclaw-pr77882`, Node v25.8.1, branch `fix/feishu-bitable-tools-gate`, commit `ccdc0366d25`.

- What was not tested: A live Feishu tenant was not exercised in this local pass; the remaining routing test should run in CI or a fully installed reviewer worktree.

- Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu Bitable/Base tools should follow the Feishu `tools` configuration gate, so installations can disable high-risk Bitable tools without disabling the Feishu channel itself.
- Real environment tested: macOS production OpenClaw host with the Feishu channel enabled and connected. Source branch under proof: `fix/feishu-bitable-tools-gate` at `0940f3814f5`.

  2. Verified live channel health: `openclaw channels status --probe --json` with account IDs/secrets redacted.
  3. Verified the PR branch exposes explicit `bitable`/`base` gating through `extensions/feishu/src/tools-config.ts` and related tests.
