target.title: feat(telegram): opt-in interleaved progress lane
target.body:
## Summary

Add an opt-in Telegram renderer that projects reasoning text and structured runtime events into a single durable live message on the reasoning lane, matching CLI ergonomics without changing execution semantics.

**Gated on three conditions:** `toolProgressEnabled && interleavedProgress === true && hasReasoningLane` — inherits every existing group/DM/visibility gate.

## What it does

When `channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` is enabled:
- Reasoning text streams into the interleaved message with a "Thinking" header
- Tool starts append a sanitized line (tool name only, never raw args by default)
- Item/plan/approval/command/patch events append title-only summary lines
- A rolling timer (20s cadence) shows elapsed time + a wall-clock stamp while running, so a stalled lane is visible at a glance; cleared on completion
- The model's reply is delivered once as the polished answer message — it is **not** mixed into the Thinking lane
- Long turns spill into continuation messages instead of freezing at Telegram's length cap
- Falls back to the existing `pushStreamToolProgress` lane when the flag is off

## Key design choices

- **Projection-only** — no changes to prompts, model routing, tool execution, or auth
- **No raw data** — tool lines show name only; `interleavedToolArgs` is a separate opt-in for sanitized args
- **No new event contract** — consumes existing `onReasoningStream`, `onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary` callbacks unchanged
- **Reasoning + tools only; reply stays out** — the lane renders reasoning (`onReasoningStream`) and tool/event lines; the model's reply prose is **not** folded into it. Folding reply text in and stripping it back out at delivery proved unreliable — the model often drafts an answer, runs tools, then re-emits a revised one, leaving several answer-like copies of which only the last matches the polished final text. So the reply is delivered exactly once, as the answer. For the interactive backend the proxy restores real thinking on its own `onReasoningStream`, so reasoning is unaffected; a defensive final-answer strip remains for redacted-thinking backends that bridge assistant text into the reasoning lane. Tagged payloads (`<think>reason</think>answer`) keep only the in-tag reasoning, so answer prose after `</think>` never lands in the lane.
- **Single reasoning stream, delta-append** — appended as monotonic deltas (a cumulative snapshot contributes only its new suffix), so re-delivery never duplicates and the lane reads chronologically reasoning → tool → reasoning → …. The overlap fold is guarded: it folds a full-tail re-send (and full-increment duplicates) but never a short/mid-word coincidence, so a fresh fragment never loses its leading character.
- **Continuation on overflow** — as the durable message approaches Telegram's 4096-char cap the lane spills into a fresh continuation message (carrying recent context) instead of letting the draft stream stop editing and freeze the lane
- **Rate-limit-aware cadence** — the rolling "still running" timer ticks every 20s (not every few seconds) so N concurrently-streaming Thinking messages stay under Telegram's per-chat edit rate and don't draw 429s (a 429 drops the edit and stalls the lane); the line carries a wall-clock stamp so a genuine stall is obvious
- **No empty Thinking** — a turn that reaches final delivery with no reasoning/tool/status content (and no running timer) renders nothing, so the lane never emits a bare "Thinking" header
- **Plain-text transcript** — reasoning tag markers (`<think>` etc.) are unwrapped so the lane shows the model's prose, never raw tags

## Prerequisite

- **#80046** (CLI tool_use event bridge with sanitized args) — **merged 2026-05-29**; supplies the `onToolStart` lifecycle events the tool lines render. This branch should be rebased onto current `main` (which now contains it) before merge.

The renderer otherwise consumes only existing callbacks and is default-off, so it depends on no unmerged work. (On the standard `claude-cli` backend, CLI-*native* reasoning text additionally benefits from #85381's `thinking_delta`; without it the lane simply shows tool/event lines. The interactive backend restores thinking via its proxy, so reasoning appears regardless. Either way the default tool-progress lane is unaffected — this is not a hard dependency.)

## Changed files

- `extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts` — pure render helpers (gate, sanitize, header-strip, message render with length cap + no-content guard + wall-clock timer stamp, delta-append with cumulative-snapshot handling + guarded overlap fold, continuation-spill computation, defensive final-answer strip, status-line append, timer-interval constant)
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` — wire callbacks through the renderer with fallback; single reasoning-stream delta append (reply prose kept out of the lane); spill into a continuation message before the length cap; suppress an empty Thinking flush at final delivery; 20s rolling-timer cadence; reasoning-tag unwrapping; clear rolling timer in `finally`
- `extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts` — `stripReasoningTagsForInterleaved` (extract only in-`<think>` reasoning, dropping answer prose after `</think>`, preserving code regions)
- `src/config/types.telegram.ts` + `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` — opt-in flag schema
- Config surface docs + bundled channel metadata regeneration

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests: disabled-by-default, explicit opt-out, tool-progress-off, no-reasoning-lane, header dedup, rolling-timer lifecycle + wall-clock stamp, whitespace-collapse + length cap, delta-append no-duplication across a long cumulative stream, guarded overlap fold (no 1-char coincidence drop; full-tail re-send still folds), continuation-spill (repeated spills stay under the cap; giant-line force-advance; oversized-body tail-cap), no-content render guard, tagged-reasoning split (answer after `</think>` dropped; streaming/code cases), defensive final-answer strip
- [x] Live Telegram proof at current head (below)

## Verification

Local checks at the current head:

```
pnpm tsgo:extensions                                                 # typecheck — clean
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts \
                 extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts \
                 extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts   # lint — clean
npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.test.ts \
               extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.test.ts   # all green
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Opt-in Telegram interleaved progress renders reasoning plus tool/progress events in one durable Thinking message, without duplicating the final answer or freezing at Telegram message limits.

Real environment tested: Real OpenClaw Telegram gateway against Telegram, default-off feature explicitly enabled, two concurrent Telegram threads in one chat.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rebuilt and ran the gateway from the PR head, enabled channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress, sent concurrent Telegram prompts that produced reasoning, tool progress, long Thinking output, and idle tool waits, then inspected Telegram messages and the gateway outbound Telegram send log.

Evidence after fix: Screenshot embedded below plus redacted outbound Telegram send-log metrics copied below: continuation msg=34783 -> msg=34786, final answer absent from every Thinking edit, zero bare Thinking sends, 20s timer edits at 04:20:15 -> 04:20:35 -> 04:20:55, and 99 outbound calls with 0 failures.

Observed result after fix: Thinking lane showed reasoning plus tool line, rolled into a continuation before the cap, sent the polished answer once as its own message, cleared empty Thinking output, and avoided Telegram 429s under the two-thread concurrent run.

What was not tested: No broad channel matrix or release package lane in this PR; focused local proof covers the Telegram renderer helpers, schema, lint, and extension typecheck.

## Live proof (current head)

Captured on the current head with the default-off feature enabled, over a **two-thread concurrent** session. The screenshot shows the visible lane; the rest is verified from the gateway's **outbound Telegram send log** — every `sendMessage` / `editMessageText` the bot issues — which shows message-level behavior a screenshot can't (message ids, edit cadence, final-state content, timing). Wire-level facts below are message-level metrics (ids, lengths, ok/timing) and contain no command/path/host content.

**Normal interleaving — reasoning + tool-use line in the Thinking lane, with the rolling timer:**

![interleaved lane](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/66da4d16b5a9467a82a65134976bc66aa956519c/proof-assets/interleaved-progress-telegram-2026-05-30-toolline.png)

**Wire-level verification (gateway send log, current head, one two-thread concurrent session):**

- **Long-message continuation** — `msg=34783` grew to the 4000-char cap and rolled into a fresh continuation `msg=34786` (carrying the next tool line) instead of freezing; clean seam, no garble.
- **No duplicate final answer** — on that long thinking-heavy turn the final answer was delivered once as its own message and is **absent from every Thinking edit** of `34783`/`34786`.
- **No empty Thinking** — **zero** bare-`Thinking`-header sends across the session.
- **20s rolling-timer cadence (rate-limit relief)** — while idling on a blocking tool, the "still running" line re-edited at **exactly 20s intervals** (04:20:15 → 04:20:35 → 04:20:55, body static at 3764 chars), keeping concurrent Thinking messages under Telegram's edit rate. The wall-clock stamp exposes any genuine stall.
- **No rate-limiting** — **99** outbound calls across the two concurrent threads, **0** failures (no `429`s, no oversized-preview stop) — the exact concurrency that previously drew 429s.

target.title: feat(doctor): add --post-upgrade --json mode for plugin-compat findings
target.body:
## Summary

Adds `openclaw doctor --post-upgrade --json`: a probe-only doctor mode that emits structured plugin-compat findings to stdout, exits non-zero when any finding has `level: "error"`. Useful for CI, post-edit smoke checks, and as the post-cutover probe consumed by external orchestrators.

Two probes ship in this PR:

- `plugin.entry_unresolved` (level: error) — fires when an enabled plugin in `installs.json` declares an `openclaw.extensions[*]` entry that doesn't resolve on disk. Catches the user-plugin class that ships a TypeScript entry without compiling.
- `plugin.manifest_drift` (level: warn) — fires when a plugin's `openclaw.plugin.json` SHA-256 differs from the `manifestHash` snapshot recorded in `installs.json`. Catches plugins whose manifest changed since the last `openclaw plugins registry --refresh`.

The runner (`runPostUpgradeProbes`) is resilient: a plugin with a missing or malformed `package.json` produces a single stderr line and is skipped, rather than crashing the whole run.

## Problem

When upgrading an OpenClaw fork between versions, plugins that previously loaded silently can stop loading without obvious failure signals: the loader skips them, the gateway boots with a smaller plugin list than expected, and downstream subscribers (e.g. diagnostic-events listeners) silently emit nothing for the missing plugins. There's no in-tree command to ask "which enabled plugins are unhealthy after my upgrade?" — operators have to grep journals or read `installs.json` by hand.

External orchestration tools (CI, custom upgrade scripts, fork-upgrade tooling) also need a structured, machine-consumable answer to that question.

## Solution

`doctor --post-upgrade --json` runs only plugin-compat probes (no health checks, no migrations, no prompts) and emits a JSON envelope:

```json
{
  "probesRun": ["plugin.entry_unresolved", "plugin.manifest_drift"],
  "findings": [{ "level": "warn|error", "code": "...", "message": "...", "plugin": "...", "entry": "..." }]
}
```

Without `--json`, the same findings render one per line as `[level] code: message`. Exit code is 1 if any finding has `level: "error"`, else 0.

The two probes are pure-filesystem: they read `installs.json` and per-plugin metadata only, no gateway RPCs, no in-process state. This keeps the surface independent of the gateway's running state and easy to invoke from CI.

## Changes

- `src/commands/doctor-post-upgrade.types.ts` — finding/report shape, probe-code const list.
- `src/commands/doctor-post-upgrade.ts` — `runPostUpgradeProbes({installsPath})` returning `PostUpgradeReport`.
- `src/commands/doctor-post-upgrade.test.ts` — three tests: entry_unresolved (fail + ok), manifest_drift (fail).
- `src/commands/doctor.types.ts` — `DoctorOptions` extended with `postUpgrade?` and `json?`.
- `src/commands/doctor.ts` — early-return branch into `runPostUpgradeProbes` when `postUpgrade` is set; preserves existing health-flow path otherwise.
- `src/cli/program/register.maintenance.ts` — `--post-upgrade` and `--json` flags wired through to `doctorCommand`.
- `CHANGELOG.md` — entry under `## Unreleased` / `### Changes`.

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** OpenClaw operators have no machine-consumable way to ask "are my installed plugins healthy after this upgrade?". CI and external upgrade tooling need a structured answer; today they have to scrape journals or re-derive the truth from `installs.json` by hand. Plugin-load regressions (entry not resolving on disk, manifest hash drift after a refresh wasn't run) surface as silent skips rather than actionable findings.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout built from this branch (`feat/doctor-post-upgrade`, head `077b7b47cb` for the changelog commit, plus the post-changelog work) on Linux 6.12.30 / Node v24.13.0. Tested against the live install state at `~/.openclaw/plugins/installs.json` on the same host (which currently has manifest drift on a handful of bundled plugins, exercising the manifest_drift probe naturally).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
pnpm install
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm test src/commands/doctor-post-upgrade.test.ts
pnpm test src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts
pnpm build
node dist/index.js doctor --post-upgrade --json
node dist/index.js doctor --post-upgrade
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

`pnpm tsgo:core` — exit 0.

`pnpm test src/commands/doctor-post-upgrade.test.ts`:
```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  3 passed (3)
```

`pnpm test src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts`:
```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  7 passed (7)
```

`pnpm build` — exit 0; `dist/` refreshed.

`node dist/index.js doctor --post-upgrade --json` (exit 0):

```json
{
  "probesRun": ["plugin.entry_unresolved", "plugin.manifest_drift"],
  "findings": [
    { "level": "warn", "code": "plugin.manifest_drift", "message": "Plugin byteplus manifest hash drifted from installs.json snapshot. Run `openclaw plugins registry --refresh` to re-sync.", "plugin": "byteplus" },
    { "level": "warn", "code": "plugin.manifest_drift", "message": "Plugin fireworks manifest hash drifted from installs.json snapshot. Run `openclaw plugins registry --refresh` to re-sync.", "plugin": "fireworks" },
    { "level": "warn", "code": "plugin.manifest_drift", "message": "Plugin google manifest hash drifted from installs.json snapshot. Run `openclaw plugins registry --refresh` to re-sync.", "plugin": "google" },
    { "level": "warn", "code": "plugin.manifest_drift", "message": "Plugin openai manifest hash drifted from installs.json snapshot. Run `openclaw plugins registry --refresh` to re-sync.", "plugin": "openai" }
  ]
}
```

`node dist/index.js doctor --post-upgrade` (exit 0):

```
[warn] plugin.manifest_drift: Plugin byteplus manifest hash drifted from installs.json snapshot. Run `openclaw plugins registry --refresh` to re-sync.
[warn] plugin.manifest_drift: Plugin fireworks manifest hash drifted from installs.json snapshot. Run `openclaw plugins registry --refresh` to re-sync.
[warn] plugin.manifest_drift: Plugin google manifest hash drifted from installs.json snapshot. Run `openclaw plugins registry --refresh` to re-sync.
[warn] plugin.manifest_drift: Plugin openai manifest hash drifted from installs.json snapshot. Run `openclaw plugins registry --refresh` to re-sync.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Both modes ran successfully against a real install. JSON output is well-formed and parseable. Human-format output matches the spec (`[level] code: message` per line). Both probes ran (`probesRun` lists both). Exit code is 0 because all findings are `warn`, not `error` — confirms the exit-code contract. The findings themselves are real: bundled plugins on the test host had manifest drift, providing organic evidence that the manifest_drift probe detects real drift in the wild.
- **What was not tested:** The `plugin.entry_unresolved` probe at error level wasn't exercised against a live install in this proof (no plugin with a broken entry is currently installed on the test host). It is exercised by the unit test (`flags an enabled plugin whose declared entry does not exist on disk`) using a fresh on-disk fixture that asserts `level: "error"`. End-to-end `pnpm check` was not run; the changed-surface gates above plus the build are the proof bar this PR carries. The two follow-up findings flagged in the spec (`plugin.silently_skipped`, `plugin.zero_diagnostic_events`) need a new gateway RPC to introspect started-plugin and listener-event state and are deferred to a follow-up PR.

## Notes

- Resilience: a plugin with a missing/malformed `package.json` produces a single stderr line and is skipped, so one broken plugin can't hide findings from other plugins.
- Skip semantics: disabled plugins are skipped entirely (consistent with how the rest of the loader treats them). Records without a `manifestHash` snapshot are skipped by the drift probe (pre-snapshot installs have nothing to compare against).
- Exit-code contract: only `level: "error"` triggers exit 1. Warnings keep exit 0 so CI can opt in to strictness via parsing the JSON output without the command itself failing.
- Hardcoded path `~/.openclaw/plugins/installs.json` for v0.1; if maintainers prefer routing through a state-dir resolver helper, happy to add it in a follow-up.

commands.author: litang9

target.title: feat(deepseek): show provider balance in usage status
target.body:
## What

- Registers DeepSeek as a provider usage source and wires the DeepSeek provider plugin to resolve API-key usage auth.
- Adds a DeepSeek balance fetcher for `GET /user/balance`, exposed through the plugin SDK provider-usage subpath.
- Adds `ProviderUsageSnapshot.summary` so balance-only providers can report provider text without pretending it is a quota window.
- Shows summary-only usage in `/status`, status reports, and the models auth status usage enrichment for DeepSeek API-key profiles.

## Why

After the provider plugin architecture moved provider-specific usage logic into plugins, DeepSeek was not wired into usage snapshots. Users could see local session costs, but `/status` and related status surfaces could not show the DeepSeek account balance.

DeepSeek balance is not a reset-window quota, so this keeps it as provider-reported summary text instead of fabricating a percent-left window.

## Public Contract Rationale

- `ProviderUsageSnapshot.summary` is optional and additive. Existing providers with quota windows keep using `windows`; shared formatters only fall back to `summary` when a provider has no windows and no error.
- `models.authStatus.usage.summary` is optional and mirrors the same snapshot field so API-key balance providers can expose account-state text without changing existing window payloads.
- The provider-usage SDK export keeps DeepSeek-specific HTTP parsing inside the bundled DeepSeek provider path instead of requiring plugins to import internal `src` modules.
- Tests cover summary-only formatting, DeepSeek auth-status enrichment, provider loading, and existing window-based formatting behavior.

## Impact

DeepSeek API-key users can now see balance text such as `Balance ¥42.50` in usage/status surfaces. Existing quota-window providers continue to use `windows`; summary-only formatting is only used when no windows are present.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: DeepSeek API-key usage snapshots now return provider balance text and the summary-only formatter displays it instead of dropping providers with no quota windows.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS checkout of this PR branch at `797c52ea95070e09c84ece1df7463e169007a68e`, Node/tsx runtime, real DeepSeek API key from local secret storage. Secret and balance amount were redacted from the captured output.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local Node/tsx snippet that imports `fetchDeepSeekUsage` from `src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.deepseek.ts`, wraps `fetch` to capture only safe request metadata, calls the live DeepSeek `https://api.deepseek.com/user/balance` endpoint with the real API key, then passes the returned snapshot through `formatUsageWindowSummary`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
DeepSeek usage live proof
head=797c52ea95070e09c84ece1df7463e169007a68e
endpoint=https://api.deepseek.com/user/balance
method=GET
authorizationHeaderPresent=true
accept=application/json
provider=deepseek
displayName=DeepSeek
windows=0
hasSummary=true
summary=Balance ¥<redacted>
formatted=Balance ¥<redacted>
error=none
```

- Observed result after fix: The live DeepSeek response produced a `ProviderUsageSnapshot` with `provider=deepseek`, zero quota windows, a non-empty balance summary, and no error. The formatter returned the same redacted balance text instead of `null`.
- What was not tested: Full gateway UI/dashboard manual click path and invalid-key live error handling were not exercised in this live proof. The exact balance amount is intentionally redacted.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This uses a real DeepSeek account balance endpoint, but the local account balance value cannot be shared publicly. Automated unit tests cover the mocked error and formatting edge cases separately.
- Before evidence: Before this patch, DeepSeek was absent from `usageProviders` and had no provider usage hook, so `/status` and usage surfaces had no DeepSeek balance snapshot to format.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.deepseek.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.format.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.load.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.shared.test.ts extensions/deepseek/index.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts`
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/concepts/usage-tracking.md docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn-applies-thinking-default.test.ts src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Current Branch Note

This branch was refreshed onto current `upstream/main` (`95890fe150`) in merge commit `e2e13557ce0d43c6943ab274dbc3a85f5ea47aed`; follow-up commits `2aa4a69943` and `797c52ea95` only align subagent config typing and clarify usage docs.

A local `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen`/commit hook attempt was blocked by registry fetch timeouts while installing newly introduced upstream binary packages. The SDK baseline was regenerated and checked directly with `node --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts`; GitHub CI will re-run the full matrix on the pushed merge head.

target.title: feat(plugin-sdk): onModelDiagnosticEvent for trusted model.* events
target.body:
## Why

Plugins subscribing to diagnostic events via `onDiagnosticEvent` (from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/diagnostic-runtime`) never see the `model.*` lifecycle. The public API filter at [`src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts:879-886`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts#L879-L886):

```ts
export function onDiagnosticEvent(listener: (evt: DiagnosticEventPayload) => void): () => void {
  return onInternalDiagnosticEvent((event, metadata) => {
    if (metadata.trusted || event.type === "log.record") {
      return;
    }
    listener(event);
  });
}
```

drops **every** event whose dispatcher metadata is `trusted: true`. The runtime emits `model.call.started`, `model.call.completed`, `model.call.error`, `model.usage`, and `model.failover` via `emitTrustedDiagnosticEvent`. So the entire model lifecycle is invisible to plugins.

This breaks observability/cost-attribution plugins. A telemetry bridge plugin needs:

- `model.call.completed` (provider/model identity) to populate per-call span attributes
- `model.usage` (token counts, cost) for per-call attribution

Today both subscriptions are dead code against the trusted emit paths. Concretely, the embedded codex/Responses runner emits the full model lifecycle via `emitTrustedDiagnosticEvent` (see `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts`), but no plugin listener ever receives any of it — model traffic on the dashboard side ends up bucketed as "unknown" instead of `gpt-5.5`/etc.

## Approach

Add a focused, opt-in plugin-sdk API `onModelDiagnosticEvent` that exposes exactly the five model lifecycle event types — and only those — to plugins. The general `onDiagnosticEvent` filter is unchanged: it still drops trusted events, so the safety contract of the broad subscription is preserved. Plugins that want model telemetry have to opt in explicitly.

The new helper otherwise mirrors `onDiagnosticEvent`'s contract (cloned-and-frozen payloads, isolated listener failures, unsubscribe disposer).

A module-local allowlist (`PLUGIN_VISIBLE_TRUSTED_MODEL_EVENT_TYPES`) documents which event types are reachable through this API and is the single place to update if more model lifecycle types are added later.

## Files

- `src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts` — add `onModelDiagnosticEvent` + allowlist (+45 lines)
- `src/plugin-sdk/{diagnostic-runtime,index,compat}.ts` — re-export the new function so plugins can `import { onModelDiagnosticEvent } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/diagnostic-runtime"` (or the top-level barrel / compat subpath)
- `src/infra/diagnostic-events.test.ts` — three new tests:
  1. delivers all five `model.*` trusted types and filters non-model traffic
  2. the original `onDiagnosticEvent` still hides model events from plugins (defends the safety contract)
  3. unsubscribe disposer stops further delivery
- `CHANGELOG.md` — Unreleased / Changes entry

Total: 6 files, +158 / −2.

## Tests

- `src/infra/diagnostic-events.test.ts`: 22/22 passing
- Plugin-SDK guardrail tests (`src/plugins/loader-sdk-import-guardrails.test.ts`, `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-root-alias.test.ts`): 44 tests, all passing

## End-to-end validation

Built a full `dist/` from this branch, swapped it into a deployed gateway, and ran a real codex (`openai-codex/gpt-5.5`) turn from a Telegram-driven session. Bridge plugin logs show **both** subscriptions on startup:

```
[agentweave-bridge] subscribed to diagnostic events via plugin-sdk
[agentweave-bridge] subscribed to model.* trusted events via plugin-sdk
```

…and during the turn the bridge now receives the `model.usage` event for codex/gpt-5.5 (previously dead-code path):

```
[agentweave-bridge] event: model.usage sessionKey: agent:main:main source: undefined
```

The downstream dashboard bucket that had been showing "unknown" for every codex turn now correctly attributes to `gpt-5.5`.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Plugins subscribing via the public `onDiagnosticEvent` API never observe trusted `model.*` lifecycle events (`model.call.started/completed/error`, `model.usage`, `model.failover`) because the existing filter at `src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts:879` drops every event whose dispatcher metadata is `trusted: true`. On my deployed install, this surfaced as Codex `gpt-5.5` turns bucketing as "unknown" in a downstream observability bridge plugin (agentweave-bridge) — the per-turn span never received `prov.llm.{provider,model}` because the bridge's `model.usage` / `model.call.completed` handlers were dead code against the trusted emit paths.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw v2026.5.10-beta.1 gateway running under `systemctl --user` on Linux (Debian 12, Node 24.13 via nvm) on my NAS (192.168.1.70), driving a Codex subscription session via Telegram channel and the embedded pi-runner. Local install built from this PR head: `dist/` produced by `pnpm build` from commit 52f87ce58a, swapped into `/home/Arnab/clawd/projects/openclaw/dist/`, gateway restarted. Bridge plugin installed under `~/.openclaw/user-plugins/agentweave-bridge/`, rebuilt against the new SDK export.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```
  # 1. Build the patched openclaw from this branch
  cd /home/Arnab/dev/openclaw-model-usage
  CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
  CI=true pnpm build

  # 2. Install over the canonical openclaw dist/
  systemctl --user stop openclaw-gateway
  mv /home/Arnab/clawd/projects/openclaw/dist /home/Arnab/clawd/projects/openclaw/dist.bak
  cp -r dist /home/Arnab/clawd/projects/openclaw/dist
  systemctl --user start openclaw-gateway

  # 3. Confirm trust-gated body in the built artifact
  grep -A2 "function onModelDiagnosticEvent" \
    /home/Arnab/clawd/projects/openclaw/dist/diagnostic-events-*.js

  # 4. Drive a real Codex turn through Telegram and observe the bridge plugin logs
  journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway -f | grep agentweave-bridge
  ```
- Evidence after fix:
  - Built-artifact contains the trust-gated callback:
    ```
    function onModelDiagnosticEvent(listener) {
      return onInternalDiagnosticEvent((event, metadata) => {
        if (!metadata.trusted) return;
    ```
  - Gateway startup logs from `journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway` show the plugin subscribing to BOTH streams (previously only one):
    ```
    [agentweave-bridge] OTel SDK started, exporting to: http://192.168.1.70:30418/v1/traces
    [agentweave-bridge] subscribed to diagnostic events via plugin-sdk
    [agentweave-bridge] subscribed to model.* trusted events via plugin-sdk
    ```
  - During a real Telegram-initiated Codex turn (openai-codex/gpt-5.5), the bridge plugin now receives a trusted `model.usage` event that was previously invisible to it:
    ```
    [agentweave-bridge] started root span for main session: agent:main:main agent: nix-v1
    [agentweave-bridge] event: model.usage sessionKey: agent:main:main source: undefined
    [agentweave-bridge] ended root span for session: agent:main:main
    ```
- Observed result after fix: The downstream observability dashboard that had been bucketing every Codex turn as "unknown" model now correctly attributes them to `gpt-5.5`, with cost figures flowing through the bridge's pricing fallback. No behavior change for any other plugin: the broad `onDiagnosticEvent` subscription still filters every trusted event as before (defended by the regression test `onDiagnosticEvent still hides model.* trusted events from plugins`). A separate regression test confirms forged untrusted `model.*` events from `emitDiagnosticEvent` do NOT reach the new opt-in subscription.
- What was not tested: I did not exercise this against an isolated subagent (`agent:isolated:*`) session, only `agent:main:main`. Did not run on macOS or Windows; tested under Linux Node 24.13 only. Did not run the live `pnpm test` matrix exhaustively — the four review-required test files all pass locally (`src/infra/diagnostic-events.test.ts` 23/23, `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts` 5/5, `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-root-alias.test.ts` 13/13, `src/plugins/loader-sdk-import-guardrails.test.ts` 8/8) plus `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` and `pnpm build` are clean.

target.title: fix(cli): harden Windows node pairing and approvals
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve stable Windows node/device identity across reconnects and same-key metadata refresh approvals, while still rotating unsafe/stale/revoked/mismatched device tokens
- harden node pairing approval authz, pending/paired node presentation, and gateway node-event routing so Windows nodes are less likely to fall back to manual/TUI repair paths
- route devices/nodes/gateway CLI RPC helpers through direct local gateway-client auth for explicit loopback shared token/password calls, without letting stale inactive env/config tokens suppress device-token fallback
- make host-native Windows exec approval get/set payloads work through the dedicated `exec.approvals.node.*` gateway methods, including native payloads that omit `payloadJSON`
- block raw `node.invoke systemExecApprovals.*` / `system.execApprovals.*` policy mutation paths and keep exec policy mutation on audited dedicated methods
- render host-native Windows node exec approval snapshots read-only in Control UI, including normalization for malformed node-supplied rules
- clarify the `nodes invoke system.run` CLI error so users are pointed to agent `/exec host=node`, not a nonexistent `openclaw exec` command
- make build/package TypeScript helper scripts run through the repo `tsx` loader so Node builds without built-in TypeScript support can complete

## Draft Snapshot
Current pushed draft head: `dd832262e54e6d0d9b2b6e5b164d5671551c4902`.

This is pushed for review now. It remains draft because the broad changed gate is still blocked by existing repo-wide oxlint debt, not by touched files in this branch.

## Proof On This Snapshot

target.title: fix(agents): report stale session locks without cleanup
target.body:
## Summary

- Split stale session-lock detection into report vs removal paths so live OpenClaw-owned stale locks surface as typed contention instead of being deleted.
- Add `SessionWriteLockStaleError` and route stale acquire failures through embedded-run takeover, failover suppression, prompt-cache propagation, announce delivery, and QA retry classifiers.
- Cover active in-process stale locks, live OpenClaw-owned stale locks, embedded post-prompt stale reacquire, and QA retry behavior.

Refs #87779

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/google-prompt-cache.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite-runtime-agent-session.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src` failed on latest `origin/main` in unrelated `src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts(984,15): Object literal may only specify known properties, and 'thinking' does not exist...`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` clean after fixing accepted QA retry finding

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: stale session transcript locks owned by a live OpenClaw process are reported as typed stale-lock contention without deleting the lock; dead/orphan reclaim remains on the existing safe removal path.
Real environment tested: local macOS checkout on branch `fix/session-stale-lock-diagnostics` after rebase onto `origin/main`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/google-prompt-cache.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite-runtime-agent-session.test.ts`.
Evidence after fix: 3 Vitest shards passed; 6 files passed; 269 tests passed.
Observed result after fix: live OpenClaw-owned stale lock test throws `SessionWriteLockStaleError` and leaves the lock file in place; QA helper retries stale lock contention.
What was not tested: live gateway multi-agent `sessions_send` / `sessions_spawn` stress run; broad `tsgo:test:src` is blocked by unrelated latest-main ACP spawn test type error noted above.

target.title: feat(deepseek): show provider balance in usage status
target.body:
## What

- Registers DeepSeek as a provider usage source and wires the DeepSeek provider plugin to resolve API-key usage auth.
- Adds a DeepSeek balance fetcher for `GET /user/balance`, exposed through the plugin SDK provider-usage subpath.
- Adds `ProviderUsageSnapshot.summary` so balance-only providers can report provider text without pretending it is a quota window.
- Shows summary-only usage in `/status`, status reports, and the models auth status usage enrichment for DeepSeek API-key profiles.

## Why

After the provider plugin architecture moved provider-specific usage logic into plugins, DeepSeek was not wired into usage snapshots. Users could see local session costs, but `/status` and related status surfaces could not show the DeepSeek account balance.

DeepSeek balance is not a reset-window quota, so this keeps it as provider-reported summary text instead of fabricating a percent-left window.

## Public Contract Rationale

- `ProviderUsageSnapshot.summary` is optional and additive. Existing providers with quota windows keep using `windows`; shared formatters only fall back to `summary` when a provider has no windows and no error.
- `models.authStatus.usage.summary` is optional and mirrors the same snapshot field so API-key balance providers can expose account-state text without changing existing window payloads.
- The provider-usage SDK export keeps DeepSeek-specific HTTP parsing inside the bundled DeepSeek provider path instead of requiring plugins to import internal `src` modules.
- Tests cover summary-only formatting, DeepSeek auth-status enrichment, provider loading, and existing window-based formatting behavior.

## Impact

DeepSeek API-key users can now see balance text such as `Balance ¥42.50` in usage/status surfaces. Existing quota-window providers continue to use `windows`; summary-only formatting is only used when no windows are present.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: DeepSeek API-key usage snapshots now return provider balance text and the summary-only formatter displays it instead of dropping providers with no quota windows.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS checkout of this PR branch at `797c52ea95070e09c84ece1df7463e169007a68e`, Node/tsx runtime, real DeepSeek API key from local secret storage. Secret and balance amount were redacted from the captured output.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local Node/tsx snippet that imports `fetchDeepSeekUsage` from `src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.deepseek.ts`, wraps `fetch` to capture only safe request metadata, calls the live DeepSeek `https://api.deepseek.com/user/balance` endpoint with the real API key, then passes the returned snapshot through `formatUsageWindowSummary`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
DeepSeek usage live proof
head=797c52ea95070e09c84ece1df7463e169007a68e
endpoint=https://api.deepseek.com/user/balance
method=GET
authorizationHeaderPresent=true
accept=application/json
provider=deepseek
displayName=DeepSeek
windows=0
hasSummary=true
summary=Balance ¥<redacted>
formatted=Balance ¥<redacted>
error=none
```

- Observed result after fix: The live DeepSeek response produced a `ProviderUsageSnapshot` with `provider=deepseek`, zero quota windows, a non-empty balance summary, and no error. The formatter returned the same redacted balance text instead of `null`.
- What was not tested: Full gateway UI/dashboard manual click path and invalid-key live error handling were not exercised in this live proof. The exact balance amount is intentionally redacted.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This uses a real DeepSeek account balance endpoint, but the local account balance value cannot be shared publicly. Automated unit tests cover the mocked error and formatting edge cases separately.
- Before evidence: Before this patch, DeepSeek was absent from `usageProviders` and had no provider usage hook, so `/status` and usage surfaces had no DeepSeek balance snapshot to format.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.deepseek.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.format.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.load.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.shared.test.ts extensions/deepseek/index.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts`
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/concepts/usage-tracking.md docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn-applies-thinking-default.test.ts src/agents/tools/gateway-tool-guard-coverage.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Current Branch Note

This branch was refreshed onto current `upstream/main` (`95890fe150`) in merge commit `e2e13557ce0d43c6943ab274dbc3a85f5ea47aed`; follow-up commits `2aa4a69943` and `797c52ea95` only align subagent config typing and clarify usage docs.

A local `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen`/commit hook attempt was blocked by registry fetch timeouts while installing newly introduced upstream binary packages. The SDK baseline was regenerated and checked directly with `node --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts`; GitHub CI will re-run the full matrix on the pushed merge head.

target.title: fix(infra): scope Windows path realpath caches
target.body:
## Summary

Windows `lstat`/realpath calls are expensive enough that repeated path-boundary checks can dominate startup and agent-path flows. The original version tried to fix that with process-wide realpath caches, but those caches sit on plugin, skill, hook, and sandbox containment checks. That is too broad: stale symlink or ancestor answers can survive after the filesystem changes.

This revision keeps the performance direction while moving the cache boundary to caller-owned operation scopes:

- `src/infra/path-safety.ts` and `src/infra/path-guards.ts` continue to expose the `@openclaw/fs-safe` caller-cache contract; no process-wide realpath cache is installed.
- `src/infra/boundary-path.ts` accepts an optional caller-owned cache for `resolvePathViaExistingAncestorSync`.
- Plugin hook/skill resolution and sandbox host-path validation now pass per-operation caches so repeated checks in one operation avoid duplicate canonicalization work.
- Regression tests assert stale symlink/junction and ancestor changes are detected without global invalidation, including native Windows via directory junctions.

Closes #85262

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: reduces repeated Windows path canonicalization in plugin hook/skill and sandbox host-path operations without adding stale process-wide path answers to security-sensitive containment checks.

Real environment tested: macOS local linked worktree for focused unit coverage; GitHub Actions `windows-2025` native Windows and WSL2 probe; GitHub Actions Windows CI `checks-windows-node-test`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/path-safety.test.ts src/infra/boundary-path.test.ts src/hooks/plugin-hooks.test.ts src/skills/loading/plugin-skills.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/host-paths.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/validate-sandbox-security.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/fs-paths.test.ts
node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/path-safety.ts src/infra/path-guards.ts src/infra/boundary-path.ts src/infra/path-safety.test.ts src/infra/boundary-path.test.ts src/hooks/plugin-hooks.ts src/skills/loading/plugin-skills.ts src/agents/sandbox/host-paths.ts src/agents/sandbox/validate-sandbox-security.ts src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.ts src/agents/sandbox/fs-paths.ts
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
```

Remote proof:

- Windows/WSL probe: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26691338015
  - native Windows: `Microsoft Windows NT 10.0.26100.0`, `arch=X64`
  - WSL2: imported `UbuntuProbe`, `Linux ... microsoft-standard-WSL2`, `PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 24.04 LTS"`, `wsl2_ok=true`
- CI Windows node lane: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26691338037/job/78668145318
- CI infra process lane: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26691338037/job/78668145571

Evidence after fix:

- Focused Vitest: 5 shards passed, 114 tests passed.
- Direct oxlint on touched files passed.
- Autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings.
- Native Windows CI node-test passed.
- Native Windows + WSL2 probe passed, including WSL2 import and execution.
- Stale symlink/junction tests now run on Windows instead of skipping.

Observed result after fix: repeated path canonicalization inside a single plugin/sandbox operation can reuse one caller-owned cache, while a later operation re-reads current filesystem state and blocks stale symlink/junction escapes.

What was not tested: a new full 120s Windows CPU profile for the scoped-cache version. The original issue profile established the `lstat` bottleneck, but the scoped version intentionally trades some of the global-cache speedup for correct cache lifetime and should be profiled before treating the exact 83% lstat reduction as reproduced.

## Known unrelated CI state

Manual broad CI on this ref currently has unrelated failures in `src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts`:

```text
src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts(844,32): error TS2345: Argument of type 'number | undefined' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.
src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts(891,28): error TS2345: Argument of type 'number | undefined' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.
```

The same failure reproduced in the remote `pnpm check:changed` gate and in CI `check-lint` / `check-prod-types`; this PR does not touch that file.

target.title: fix(voice-call): avoid OpenAI realtime double greeting
target.body:
Fixes #85846

## Summary

- Keep OpenAI outbound queued initial greetings on the active explicit ready-trigger path so the provider still sends `conversation.item.create` followed by `response.create` after `session.updated`.
- Suppress OpenAI Realtime server-VAD auto responses while a manual response is being created, then restore the original auto-response behavior on `response.done`, `response.cancelled`, and response error paths.
- Preserve base realtime session instructions instead of moving the queued greeting into `session.update` instructions, addressing the lost-initial-response risk called out in review.
- Keep non-OpenAI realtime providers on the existing explicit on-ready initial greeting path.
- No config surface or plugin API surface changed.

## Behavior proof

The updated focused regressions now cover the OpenAI outbound queued-greeting split and ClawSweeper's one-response-source concern:

- `createBridge(...).instructions` remains the base session instructions (`"Be helpful."`), so the initial greeting is not passively parked in `session.update`.
- `onReady()` calls `triggerGreeting()` exactly once with the full queued first-turn instruction.
- The OpenAI provider regression proves `triggerGreeting()` sends one `conversation.item.create`, temporarily updates turn detection to `create_response:false`, sends one `response.create`, then restores `create_response:true` after the manual response finishes.
- A response-error regression proves the temporary suppression is restored if OpenAI rejects the manual response request.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 2 files, 71 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts` -> 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

target.title: fix(diagnostics): carry session UUID on interactive dispatch events
target.body:
## Why

Plugins and exporters that subscribe to diagnostic events via `onDiagnosticEvent` need the canonical session UUID — the same id OpenClaw's JSONL session transcript writes as its session-header `id` field (`src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/bootstrap.ts`) — so downstream systems can join diagnostic/telemetry rows back to the transcript by id.

The **cron / isolated-agent** path already provides it: `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts` passes `sessionId: prepared.context.runSessionId` into `createDiagnosticMessageLifecycle`, so `message.queued` and `session.state` events carry the UUID.

The **interactive dispatch** path does not. `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` creates the diagnostic lifecycle with `sessionKey` only — even though the canonical UUID is available from the session store entry (`resolveSessionStoreLookup(ctx, cfg).entry?.sessionId`, the same source already used a few lines later to resolve `operationSessionId`). As a result every interactive `message.queued` / `session.state` event emits `sessionId: undefined`, and consumers cannot join those events to the transcript by UUID.

This is an asymmetry/oversight, not a design choice — there is no config that turns it on.

## Change

Pass `sessionId: initialSessionStoreEntry.entry?.sessionId` into the interactive `createDiagnosticMessageLifecycle({...})` call. The `resolveSessionStoreLookup(ctx, cfg)` lookup (read-only; needs only `ctx` + `cfg`, both already in scope) is hoisted a few lines up so its result is available at lifecycle-creation time. The lifecycle's existing `resolveRef` then defaults `sessionId` onto every emitted event (`message.queued`, `session.state`, `message.processed`), matching the cron path.

Safety: when the store entry exists, `entry?.sessionId` equals the UUID written to the JSONL session header. When it is absent (brand-new session not yet persisted), it is `undefined`, which preserves today's `sessionKey`-only behavior — the change never emits a *mismatched* UUID. This mirrors the existing canonical resolution (`operationSessionId = ... ?? initialSessionStoreEntry.entry?.sessionId ?? ...`).

## Files

- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` — hoist `initialSessionStoreEntry` lookup above the lifecycle creation; pass `sessionId` (+2/−1).
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` — new test asserting interactive diagnostic events carry the session-store UUID.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** On the interactive dispatch path, diagnostic events (`message.queued`, `session.state`, `message.processed`) were emitted with `sessionId: undefined`, so a plugin subscribing via `onDiagnosticEvent` could not join them to the JSONL session transcript by UUID. After this patch they carry the canonical session UUID, matching the cron path.

**Real environment tested:** A deployed OpenClaw gateway (v2026.4.29) built from this branch on a Linux host, running a live Telegram channel with an `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` agent. Diagnostic spans exported via OTLP through a running OpenTelemetry collector into Grafana Tempo.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Built the runtime from this branch into the gateway `dist/`, restarted the gateway service, sent a real message to the agent over Telegram (session `agent:main:main`), then queried the trace store with `curl`:
`curl -sG "$TEMPO/api/search" --data-urlencode 'q={ .prov.harness="openclaw" && .prov.session.uuid != "" }'`, then fetched the returned trace and read its span attributes. Gateway events were read live with `journalctl -u openclaw-gateway -f`.

**After-fix evidence:** Live runtime output (redacted). The interactive turn ran its full lifecycle in the gateway log:
```
[agentweave-bridge] event: message.queued      sessionKey: agent:main:main source: dispatch
[agentweave-bridge] event: model.call.started  sessionKey: agent:main:main
[agentweave-bridge] event: model.call.completed sessionKey: agent:main:main
[agentweave-bridge] event: model.usage         sessionKey: agent:main:main
[agentweave-bridge] event: message.processed    sessionKey: agent:main:main
```
The resulting `openclaw.turn` span, fetched from Tempo (`curl .../api/traces/<id>`), now carries the UUID — it carried none on this path before the patch:
```
span: openclaw.turn
  prov.harness      = openclaw
  prov.session.id   = agent:main:main                       (route key, unchanged)
  prov.session.key  = agent:main:main
  prov.session.uuid = 4767da88-20d1-459d-b1d6-60ecd493b0fa   (canonical session UUID, now present)
```
`4767da88-…` is the same UUID OpenClaw writes to the JSONL session-header `id`, so the diagnostic stream / span can now be joined to the transcript by id.

**Observed result after fix:** The interactive turn completed normally and its diagnostic events + telemetry span carry `sessionId` / `prov.session.uuid` equal to the canonical session UUID. Before the patch this field was absent on the interactive path; `prov.session.id` (the route key) is unchanged, so parent-linking is unaffected. No other behavior changed.

**What was not tested:** The brand-new-session case where no store entry exists yet — the lookup returns `undefined` and the event keeps today's `sessionKey`-only behavior. That path is covered by the added unit test but was not exercised against the live gateway.

## Tests

New test `carries the session store UUID on interactive diagnostic events`: drives a real interactive (Slack) dispatch with a store entry whose `sessionId` is `test-uuid-1234`, and asserts the emitted `logMessageQueued` and `logSessionStateChange` both carry `sessionId: "test-uuid-1234"`. Red before the change, green after. Full `dispatch-from-config.test.ts` suite remains green. (Supplemental to the live proof above.)

## Scope

Independent of #80497 (which adds `onModelDiagnosticEvent` at the delivery/filter layer in `src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts`). This PR is at the emit/population layer (`dispatch-from-config.ts`) and touches disjoint files; the two can land in either order.

target.title: fix(codex): restore bounded recovery continuity
target.body:
## Summary

- restore bounded OpenClaw-visible continuity for Codex native recovery paths: fresh/transient starts and stale bindings with newer visible messages
- keep healthy native resumes from replaying mirrored transcript history, including messages already covered by a successful native turn
- keep post-start continuity as turn input only so native Codex thread developer instructions stay stable
- filter only Codex app-server mirror echoes, not other backend mirror identities

## Codex source checked

- `../codex/codex-rs/app-server-protocol/src/protocol/v2/thread.rs:95` and `../codex/codex-rs/app-server-protocol/src/protocol/v2/thread.rs:330`: `thread/start` and `thread/resume` carry `developer_instructions`.
- `../codex/codex-rs/app-server-protocol/src/protocol/v2/turn.rs:66`: `turn/start` carries `input` and turn settings, not thread-level developer instructions.
- `../codex/codex-rs/app-server/src/request_processors/thread_processor.rs:830` and `../codex/codex-rs/app-server/src/request_processors/thread_processor.rs:2474`: start/resume map developer instructions into thread config overrides.
- `../codex/codex-rs/app-server/src/request_processors/turn_processor.rs:413`: turn input maps into `Op::UserInput`.
- `../codex/codex-rs/core/src/session/mod.rs:573` and `../codex/codex-rs/core/src/session/mod.rs:2692`: Codex stores thread developer instructions in session config and emits them as developer messages.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --prompt "..."` clean after fixing accepted findings

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex app-server recovery paths could lose OpenClaw-visible continuity when no active native Codex thread already covered the visible transcript, while healthy native resumes must still avoid duplicated mirrored history.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout with mocked Codex app-server lifecycle tests exercising `thread/start`, `thread/resume`, `turn/start`, binding rotation, mirrored transcript filtering, and successful-turn binding coverage.

Observed result after fix: fresh native starts receive bounded continuity; stale bindings receive only newer visible messages; active-turn persisted messages are not replayed on the next resume; Codex app-server mirror echoes are suppressed without suppressing other backend mirror identities; thread-start developer instructions remain stable after native start.

What was not tested: packaged app-server binary or live upstream model completion.

Closes #88352.
Closes #88354.

target.title: fix(infra): prefix channelId with channel: when migrating to target
target.body:
## Summary
- Normalize legacy Discord `channelId` targets by converting bare ids on `to`-mode message actions to `channel:<id>`.
- Preserve explicitly-prefixed targets (`channel:`, `dm:`, `group:`, `thread:`, `user:`) and keep `channelId`-mode actions unchanged.
- Keep non-Discord legacy `channelId` values raw so provider-specific target grammars such as Telegram topics and group ids are not rewritten.

## Root Cause
The message-action normalizer accepted legacy `channelId` fields in places where newer outbound routing expects recipient-shaped targets. For Discord send/edit/delete style actions, a bare channel snowflake can be ambiguous unless it is normalized to an explicit `channel:` recipient. The first version of this PR applied that rewrite to all providers, which was too broad because non-Discord providers can use their own channel id grammar.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message-action-normalization.test.ts`
  - Test Files: 1 passed
  - Tests: 22 passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/outbound/message-action-normalization.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-normalization.test.ts`
  - no warnings/errors
- `git diff --check`
  - clean

## Regression Coverage
- Discord `send` with legacy bare `channelId` now resolves to `channel:<id>`.
- Discord current-provider fallback still prefixes bare legacy channel ids.
- Telegram topic-style and group-prefixed legacy `channelId` values stay unchanged.

## Live Proof
Still pending: a live Discord send/edit/delete path against a staging gateway or controlled test channel.

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened because two independent delivery paths were active simultaneously:

1. **Draft stream path**: `onPartialReply` → `bot.api.sendMessage()` sent preview text immediately
2. **ACP projector path**: `suppressBlockUserDelivery` blocked ACP block delivery but couldn't reach the upstream draft stream
3. **TTS finalization**: voice note with caption sent after synthesis, then draft text deleted

This PR eliminates the churn by suppressing draft stream creation at source when captioned final TTS will be used.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. onPartialReply fires → draft stream creates sendMessage preview → user sees text
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered → draft text deleted
  Result: user sees text flash then disappear = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. resolveCaptionedFinalTtsPlan() → willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true
  2. canStreamAnswerDraft = false → NO draft stream created
  3. TTS synthesis runs silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/tts/captioned-final-tts.ts` | **New** shared helper `resolveCaptionedFinalTtsPlan()` combining channel capability, TTS mode, and TTS status checks |
| `src/tts/captioned-final-tts.test.ts` | **New** 4 unit tests for the shared helper |
| `src/plugin-sdk/tts-runtime.ts` | Re-export `resolveCaptionedFinalTtsPlan` for extension access |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | Refactored to use shared helper instead of inline TTS logic; removed `channelTtsCapabilitiesRuntimeLoader` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | 4 regression tests for deferred settlement, block suppression, abort fallback |
| `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` | Added `isTelegramInboundAudioContext()`, `resolveTelegramSessionTtsAuto()`, and `!willUseCaptionedFinalTts` guard on `canStreamAnswerDraft` |
| `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` | 2 new tests: draft suppression when captioned TTS active, normal streaming when not |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | Block delivery suppression support |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | Delivery coordinator for suppressed text fallback |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 58 passed (58)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tts/captioned-final-tts.test.ts
 ✓ 4 passed (4)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
 ✓ 96 passed (96)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

$ pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check
 OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256

Total: 170 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode "delete-and-resend churn" — when TTS mode is `final`, users saw a text message flash briefly (2-5 seconds), then it disappeared and a voice note with caption appeared. Root cause: two independent delivery paths (draft stream via `onPartialReply` and ACP block suppression via `suppressBlockUserDelivery`) operated at different layers, so the draft stream was never suppressed.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `e33c474a`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`, `messages.tts.auto: "always"`, `channels.telegram.streaming.mode: "block"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/48AAB56AD0D1DC685CB3918F75AEB402)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message ("老板好，好久不见！有什么需要效劳的？") AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption. This is the churn behavior described in #83511: the draft stream sends text via `sendMessage` immediately, then TTS synthesis completes and sends a separate voice note.

- **Exact steps after this patch**:
  1. Applied patch (commit `e33c474a`) via `npm link`
  2. Restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Sent messages from Telegram to the bot
  4. Observed: voice note with caption appeared directly — no text flash

- **Evidence after fix (live Telegram screenshot)**:

  ![After fix — voice messages with caption, no text churn](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/6937B66CC8A27C33B76FBD54155B542C)

  The screenshot shows two voice messages from the bot, each with text caption in the same bubble. No separate text messages were sent — the text is delivered as caption on the voice note, exactly as intended.

- **Observed result after fix**: Voice notes are delivered as a single message with text caption. No draft text message is created or deleted. The `canStreamAnswerDraft` flag is correctly set to `false` when captioned final TTS is planned, preventing draft stream creation at source. Non-TTS conversations continue to use normal streaming. Other channels (Discord, Slack, etc.) are unaffected.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS behavior (tested in DM only). The `auto=inbound` edge case was verified by unit tests but not live-tested.

Closes #83511


---

## Additional fix: dispatch-from-config text deferral + draft stream suppression (e33c474a)

The original fix addressed only `dispatch-acp.ts` and `bot-message-dispatch.ts`. Live testing revealed two additional issues:

### Issue 1: Missing text deferral in `dispatch-from-config.ts`
The user's agent used the `dispatch-from-config` path (not ACP), which lacked the `shouldDeferTextForCaptionedFinalTts` gate. Block text was delivered before TTS voice synthesis completed, causing split messages.

**Fix**: Added `shouldDeferTextForCaptionedFinalTts` gate in `dispatch-from-config.ts` with:
- Block text accumulation and deferral
- Final payload text merging when resolver returns text
- Deferred text recovery on abort/error paths via `routeReplyToOriginating`
- 8 targeted tests (189 total in dispatch-from-config.test.ts)

### Issue 2: Draft stream (`onPartialReply`) operating independently
The Telegram draft stream sent real-time text previews via `editMessageText` independently from block delivery suppression, causing text to flicker before voice+caption arrived.

**Fix**: Suppress `onPartialReply` when `shouldDeferTextForCaptionedFinalTts` is active:
```typescript
onPartialReply: shouldDeferTextForCaptionedFinalTts
  ? undefined
  : wrapProgressCallback(params.replyOptions?.onPartialReply),
```

### Additional files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | Text deferral gate + draft stream suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | 9 new tests for deferral, merge, abort, error, routing, and draft suppression |
| `src/tts/tts-config.ts` | Type definitions for captioned TTS |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability |
| `extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery-text-deliverer.ts` | TTS voice caption merge in lane deliverer |
| `extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts` | 2 new tests for lane deliverer merge path |

### Verified fix (e33c474a)

![After fix — single voice message with caption, no text flicker](https://cdn.deepminer.com.cn/im-test-xming/chat/5/f6138257d34c4372bf6b6143e44cb3f5____2060648631760850944/A1CC95B74A5340F384CAB69EDB482F34.jpg)

### Test results (all passing)

```
dispatch-from-config.test.ts:     189 passed
dispatch-from-config.acp-abort:    16 passed
dispatch-acp.test.ts:              59 passed
lane-delivery.test.ts:             34 passed
Total:                            298 passed
```

target.title: fix(agents): scale read output for small contexts
target.body:
This keeps default `read` output smaller for small-context local models without changing explicit caller limits. It is part of the small-model stability work: smaller models were getting oversized file reads by default, then losing useful context or stalling around follow-up tool use.

Refs #86599.

## Summary

- Scale the adaptive default `read` byte cap from the active model context window, with an 8KB floor and 128KB ceiling.
- Preserve explicit `limit` values, including `0` and negative values that the base read tool normalizes to one line.
- Keep continuation guidance inside the byte budget and avoid misleading offsets when the first line or trailing whitespace-only lines hit the cap.
- Add focused coverage for small-context caps, long lines, explicit limits, whitespace-only capped content, EOF pagination, and continuation-text-in-file false positives.

## Verification

Latest pushed head: `d62ff11f834` on base `94b1427fdfa`.

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed after rebase.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts` passed after rebase.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts` passed after rebase.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed after rebase: 1 Vitest shard, 15 tests.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` passed clean after rebase: no accepted/actionable findings, overall 0.84.
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed before the final rebase with exit 0: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_4b41ba6b3f30`, run `run_4d8458e94046`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`, lease stopped.
- AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` was rerun after rebasing onto `94b1427fdfa`; the touched core/typecheck lanes passed, then the gate failed during `lint core changed files` while preparing Discord boundary DTS for an unrelated current-main error in `extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts:165`. Run: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_f850f1316358`, run `run_d1a10af9276d`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, lease stopped. The failure is from current main commit `94b1427fdfa` and is not in this PR's diff.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: small-context models get a smaller default file-read payload, while explicit caller limits keep the existing read contract.

Real environment tested: local focused node-wrapper tests for read-tool behavior; AWS Crabbox Linux changed-gate runner.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`; `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 180m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`.

Evidence after fix: focused read-tool tests passed on pushed SHA `d62ff11f834`; AWS Crabbox `run_4d8458e94046` completed with exit 0 before the final rebase and covered the `core`/`coreTests` lanes; post-rebase AWS Crabbox `run_d1a10af9276d` reached the unrelated current-main Discord boundary DTS failure after core typecheck and core-test typecheck passed.

Observed result after fix: adaptive reads cap output for small context windows, explicit limits still delegate to the base read tool, capped whitespace-only pages keep emitted content aligned with continuation offsets, and continuation-looking file text is not treated as pagination metadata.

What was not tested: real Ollama/model-provider live chat; this PR changes read-tool context sizing, not provider streaming.

## Summary

- replaces the reported plugin/tool fixture names in schema-quarantine, doctor, bundled MCP, Codex dynamic-tool, and gateway protocol tests with neutral `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` style names
- keeps expected warnings, diagnostic ids, paths, allowlists, and targets aligned with the renamed fixtures
- leaves production behavior untouched; this is test fixture hygiene for the runtime schema hardening work

## Verification

- `git grep -n "<reported fixture name>" HEAD -- 'src/**/*.test.ts' 'test/**/*.test.ts' 'extensions/**/*.test.ts' 'packages/**/*.test.ts'`: no matches
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/agents-models-skills.test.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/active-tool-schema-warnings.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: test fixtures no longer expose the originally reported private plugin/tool names while preserving coverage for unsupported runtime tool schema warnings and quarantine behavior.
Real environment tested: local focused Vitest/format/lint/review proof plus remote Linux changed gate through AWS Crabbox.

What was not tested: runtime behavior changes; none are intended in this test-only PR.

commands.author: stevenepalmer

target.title: fix(configure): migrate stale Codex defaults after OpenAI auth
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Fixes a configure/auth mismatch where OpenAI ChatGPT/Codex auth could preserve a stale legacy `codex/gpt-5.5` default while adding the canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` model.
- Keeps the selected primary model aligned with the canonical OpenAI provider id returned by auth.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #88456 reports successful ChatGPT/Codex device pairing followed by config returning to the menu with an unusable default model outside the selected provider model set.

What is the intended outcome?

- After OpenAI/Codex auth returns `openai/gpt-5.5`, a matching stale `codex/gpt-5.5` or `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` primary is migrated to `openai/gpt-5.5` instead of being preserved.
- Existing default preservation is unchanged for unrelated providers.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- This does not redesign the OpenAI/Codex model picker or change live OAuth/device-code transport.
- This does not remove legacy model-map entries; it only prevents a matching stale legacy ref from remaining the primary default.

What does success look like?

- The configured primary model is present under the canonical OpenAI model entry after auth.
- Existing non-OpenAI defaults are still preserved when provider auth only adds another available model.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The preserve-existing-default guard in `src/plugins/provider-auth-choice.ts`.
- The regression test proving `codex/gpt-5.5` migrates to `openai/gpt-5.5` only for the matching OpenAI auth path.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #88456

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #88456

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- No direct maintainer request beyond the linked issue.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: OpenAI/Codex auth no longer leaves a matching stale legacy Codex ref as the default primary while canonical OpenAI refs are added to the model map.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on this branch, invoking the actual provider-auth config application path with a redacted local token/provider setup.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the auth-choice config application path directly with before primary `codex/gpt-5.5`, selected auth model `openai/gpt-5.5`, and `preserveExistingDefaultModel: true`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  Terminal transcript:
  ```text
  $ COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.cache/corepack PNPM_HOME=/home/node/.local/share/pnpm node --import tsx --input-type=module
  {
    "beforePrimary": "codex/gpt-5.5",
    "afterPrimary": "openai/gpt-5.5",
    "allowlistKeys": [
      "codex/gpt-5.5",
      "openai/gpt-5.5"
    ],
    "note": {
      "title": "Model configured",
      "message": "Default model set to openai/gpt-5.5"
    }
  }
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The primary default changes to canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` while both legacy and canonical model entries remain present in `agents.defaults.models`.
- What was not tested: A full live ChatGPT/Codex device-code browser pairing against a real account from this container.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The worker cannot complete the interactive account authorization step without a user/browser handoff. The proof exercises the same config mutation path after auth returns a canonical OpenAI default.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The linked issue shows the broken state: primary `codex/gpt-5.5` with canonical OpenAI entries in the model allowlist.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts src/commands/auth-choice.apply.plugin-provider.test.ts src/commands/configure.gateway-auth.prompt-auth-config.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugins/provider-auth-choice.ts src/commands/auth-choice.apply.plugin-provider.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added provider-auth coverage for `preserveExistingDefaultModel: true` with existing primary `codex/gpt-5.5` and OpenAI auth default `openai/gpt-5.5`.
- The test verifies the primary is migrated to `openai/gpt-5.5`, the canonical model entry is present, and the provider selection hook still runs.

What failed before this fix, if known?
